It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in [[1943]]. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: Annual Festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. {{Shrine | Name = Sana Shrine | Image = [[File:Sana Shrine.jpg|250px]] | Location = [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki]], [[Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], Nita 156 | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|51.3|N|136|32|46.1|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Enshrined Deities = [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]]
[[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha (Minor)]], Former Prefectural Shrine | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = [[Shimmei-zukuri]] | Alternate Name = Nakamiya, Ōmori Shrine | Annual Festival = [[October 8]] | Rituals = }} '''Sana Shrine''' (佐那神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki District]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine has historical ties to the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] and is associated with [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]], who is also enshrined as a [[secondary deity]] at the Ise Grand Shrine. == Name and Etymology == The name "Sana" originates from the place name "Sana-no-agata" mentioned in ancient texts like the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Kotaijingu Gishikicho]]''.''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 411. In the [[Edo period]], the shrine was popularly called "Ōmori Shrine" or "Nakamiya." The name "Nakamiya" is believed to relate to its status as one of three prominent shrines in the region, alongside Sumaru-me Shrine (Ichinomiya) and [[Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)|Kushida Shrine]] (Taisha). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the following two deities: * [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]] * [[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] Ame-no-Tajikarao is mentioned in the ''Kojiki'' as residing in "Sana-no-agata," while Akebono-Tatsu-O is described as the ancestor of the [[Sana clan]]. Additional deities from merged shrines include: * [[Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]] * [[Izanami-no-Mikoto]] * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Susanoo]] * [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] * [[Ame-no-Hohi]] * [[Amatsuhikone]] * [[Ikutsuhikone]] * [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano-Kusubi]] * [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Tagorihime]] * [[Ichikishimahime]] * [[Kagutsuchi]] * [[Ukanomitama]] * [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]] * [[Konohanasakuya-hime]] * [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Koto-shiro-nushi]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927) as a minor shrine (小社).''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 412. The shrine's main hall has historically been rebuilt using materials donated from the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] during its [[Shikinen Sengu|Shikinen Sengu]] reconstruction. In the Edo period, the shrine received official recognition as a ''Shikinaisha'' and was referred to as "Sana Shrine" on a stone marker erected in [[1724]]. It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in [[1943]]. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: Annual Festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from [[Matsusaka]], [[Kameyama]], and as far as [[Nagoya]] and [[Osaka]].Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[JR Central|JR]] [[Kisei Main Line]] [[Sana Station]], approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. {{Shrine | Name = Sana Shrine | image = [[File:Sana Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location = [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki]], [[Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], Nita 156 | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|51.3|N|136|32|46.1|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Enshrined Deities = [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]]
[[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] | type = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha (Minor)]], Former Prefectural Shrine | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = [[Shimmei-zukuri]] | Alternate Name = Nakamiya, Ōmori Shrine | festival = [[October 8]] | Rituals = }} '''Sana Shrine''' (佐那神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki District]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine has historical ties to the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] and is associated with [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]], who is also enshrined as a [[secondary deity]] at the Ise Grand Shrine. == Name and Etymology == The name "Sana" originates from the place name "Sana-no-agata" mentioned in ancient texts like the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Kotaijingu Gishikicho]]''.''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 411. In the [[Edo period]], the shrine was popularly called "Ōmori Shrine" or "Nakamiya." The name "Nakamiya" is believed to relate to its status as one of three prominent shrines in the region, alongside Sumaru-me Shrine (Ichinomiya) and [[Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)|Kushida Shrine]] (Taisha). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the following two deities: * [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]] * [[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] Ame-no-Tajikarao is mentioned in the ''Kojiki'' as residing in "Sana-no-agata," while Akebono-Tatsu-O is described as the ancestor of the [[Sana clan]]. Additional deities from merged shrines include: * [[Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]] * [[Izanami-no-Mikoto]] * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Susanoo]] * [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] * [[Ame-no-Hohi]] * [[Amatsuhikone]] * [[Ikutsuhikone]] * [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano-Kusubi]] * [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Tagorihime]] * [[Ichikishimahime]] * [[Kagutsuchi]] * [[Ukanomitama]] * [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]] * [[Konohanasakuya-hime]] * [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Koto-shiro-nushi]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927) as a minor shrine (小社).''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 412. The shrine's main hall has historically been rebuilt using materials donated from the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] during its [[Shikinen Sengu|Shikinen Sengu]] reconstruction. In the Edo period, the shrine received official recognition as a ''Shikinaisha'' and was referred to as "Sana Shrine" on a stone marker erected in [[1724]]. It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in [[1943]]. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from [[Matsusaka]], [[Kameyama]], and as far as [[Nagoya]] and [[Osaka]].Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[JR Central|JR]] [[Kisei Main Line]] [[Sana Station]], approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. It is also accessible via [[Mie Kotsu]] bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Taki, Mie]] * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en}}[[ja:佐那神社]] ({{ill|Taki District|en}}[[ja:多気郡]], {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}[[ja:三重県]]) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en}}[[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en}}[[ja:尼崎市]], {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en}}[[ja:兵庫県]]) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en}}[[ja:雄山神社]] ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en}}[[ja:立山町]], {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en}}[[ja:上新川郡]], {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en}}[[ja:富山県]]) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Tomei Shrine|en}}[[ja:戸明神社]] ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en}}[[ja:北九州市]], {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en}}[[ja:福岡県]]) disambiguation]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sana Shrine}}[[Tajikarao Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines by Deity]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sana Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tajikarao Shrine]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refresh]] [[Category:Tajikarao Shrine]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[ja:手力雄神社]]

It is also accessible via [[Mie Kotsu]] bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Taki, Mie]] * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en}}[[ja:佐那神社]] ({{ill|Taki District|en}}[[ja:多気郡]], {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}[[ja:三重県]]) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en}}[[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en}}[[ja:尼崎市]], {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en}}[[ja:兵庫県]]) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en}}[[ja:雄山神社]] ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en}}[[ja:立山町]], {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en}}[[ja:上新川郡]], {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en}}[[ja:富山県]]) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Tomei Shrine|en}}[[ja:戸明神社]] ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en}}[[ja:北九州市]], {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en}}[[ja:福岡県]]) disambiguation]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sana Shrine}}[[Tajikarao Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines by Deity]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sana Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tajikarao Shrine]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refresh]] [[Category:Tajikarao Shrine]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[ja:手力雄神社]]

revision.comment: adding religious affiliation to the ones with a broken religious affiliation section
revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Sana Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sana Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location = [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki]], [[Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], Nita 156 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|51.3|N|136|32|46.1|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Enshrined Deities = [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]]
[[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] | type = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha (Minor)]], Former Prefectural Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = [[Shimmei-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Nakamiya, Ōmori Shrine | festival = [[October 8]] | Rituals = }} '''Sana Shrine''' (佐那神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki District]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine has historical ties to the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] and is associated with [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]], who is also enshrined as a [[secondary deity]] at the Ise Grand Shrine. == name and Etymology == The name "Sana" originates from the place name "Sana-no-agata" mentioned in ancient texts like the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Kotaijingu Gishikicho]]''.''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 411. In the [[Edo period]], the shrine was popularly called "Ōmori Shrine" or "Nakamiya." The name "Nakamiya" is believed to relate to its status as one of three prominent shrines in the region, alongside Sumaru-me Shrine (Ichinomiya) and [[Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)|Kushida Shrine]] (Taisha). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the following two deities: * [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]] * [[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] Ame-no-Tajikarao is mentioned in the ''Kojiki'' as residing in "Sana-no-agata," while Akebono-Tatsu-O is described as the ancestor of the [[Sana clan]]. Additional deities from merged shrines include: * [[Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]] * [[Izanami-no-Mikoto]] * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Susanoo]] * [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] * [[Ame-no-Hohi]] * [[Amatsuhikone]] * [[Ikutsuhikone]] * [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano-Kusubi]] * [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Tagorihime]] * [[Ichikishimahime]] * [[Kagutsuchi]] * [[Ukanomitama]] * [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]] * [[Konohanasakuya-hime]] * [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Koto-shiro-nushi]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927) as a minor shrine (小社).''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 412. The shrine's main hall has historically been rebuilt using materials donated from the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] during its [[Shikinen Sengu|Shikinen Sengu]] reconstruction. In the Edo period, the shrine received official recognition as a ''Shikinaisha'' and was referred to as "Sana Shrine" on a stone marker erected in [[1724]]. It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in [[1943]]. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from [[Matsusaka]], [[Kameyama]], and as far as [[Nagoya]] and [[Osaka]].Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[JR Central|JR]] [[Kisei Main Line]] [[Sana Station]], approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. It is also accessible via [[Mie Kotsu]] bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Taki, Mie]] * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en}}[[ja:佐那神社]] ({{ill|Taki District|en}}[[ja:多気郡]], {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}[[ja:三重県]]) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en}}[[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en}}[[ja:尼崎市]], {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en}}[[ja:兵庫県]]) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en}}[[ja:雄山神社]] ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en}}[[ja:立山町]], {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en}}[[ja:上新川郡]], {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en}}[[ja:富山県]]) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Tomei Shrine|en}}[[ja:戸明神社]] ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en}}[[ja:北九州市]], {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en}}[[ja:福岡県]]) disambiguation]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sana Shrine}}[[Tajikarao Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines by Deity]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sana Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tajikarao Shrine]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refresh]] [[Category:Tajikarao Shrine]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[ja:手力雄神社]]

revision.comment: 外部リンクの修正 (iss.ndl.go.jp) ([[Wikipedia:Bot|Bot]]による編集)
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 佐那神社 |画像= [[File:Sana Shrine.jpg|250px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[多気郡]][[多気町]]仁田156番地 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 51.3 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 32 | 経度秒 = 46.1 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[アメノタヂカラオ|天手力男命]]
[[曙立王|曙立王命]] |社格= [[式内社]]（小）
旧県社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 中宮、大森社 |札所等= |例祭= [[10月8日]] |神事= }} '''佐那神社'''（さなじんじゃ）は、[[三重県]][[多気郡]][[多気町]]仁田にある[[神社]]。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。 [[伊勢神宮]][[皇大神宮]]（内宮）に[[相殿神]]として祀られている[[アメノタヂカラオ|天手力男命]]を主[[祭神]]とし、伊勢神宮の古材の払い下げを受けて[[社殿]]が造り替えられるなど、伊勢神宮との関連の深い神社である式内社研究会 編（1990）：411 - 413ページ。 == 社名 == 社名の「佐那」は、『[[古事記]]』や『皇太神宮儀式帳』に記載のある「佐那県」という[[地名]]に由来する式内社研究会 編（1990）：411ページ。また近世には鎮座地周辺を「佐奈谷」と称していた。[[江戸時代]]には俗称として「大森社」・「中の宮」と呼ばれていた。『[[勢陽五鈴遺響]]』は「佐'''奈'''神社」と記す平凡社（1983）：585ページ。「中の宮」の名は、佐那神社とともに神宮造替使によって社殿の建て替えが行われた多気郡の3社である須麻漏賣社を一之大宮、[[櫛田神社 (松阪市)|櫛田神社]]を大社と呼んだことと関連すると考えられる。 == 祭神 == [[アメノタヂカラオ|天手力男命]]と[[曙立王|曙立王命]]を主祭神とする。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に「佐那神社二座」とあるように、2柱の[[神 (神道)|神]]を祀り、うち1柱を天手力男命とすることは諸書で概ね一致するが、もう1柱については、曙立王命のほか若沙那賣神、[[天石門別神|天石窓神]]、御代宿禰とする説もある式内社研究会 編（1990）：411 - 412ページ。天手力男命は『古事記』の[[天孫降臨]]の段に「手力男神者、坐'''佐那那県'''也」と記され、曙立王命は『古事記』の[[開化天皇]]の段に「此曙立王者、伊勢之品遅部君、伊勢之'''佐那造'''之祖」と記されていることが佐那神社の祭神たる根拠となっている。 上記の2柱のほか、合祀元の神社の祭神であった[[アメノウズメ|天宇受賣命]]・[[熊野権現|速玉男命]]・[[イザナミ|伊邪那美命]]・[[アマテラス|天照大御神]]・[[スサノオ|須佐之男命]]・[[アメノオシホミミ|天忍穂耳命]]・[[アメノホヒ|天穂日命]]・[[アマツヒコネ|天津日子根命]]・[[イクツヒコネ|活津日子根命]]・[[クマノクスビ|熊野久須毘命]]・[[タキリビメ|多紀理毘賣命]]・[[タギツヒメ|多岐都比賣命]]・[[イチキシマヒメ|市寸嶋比賣命]]・[[カグツチ|火産霊神]]・[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]]・[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]]・[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花咲耶姫命]]・[[熊野権現|事解男命]]・[[応神天皇|誉田別尊]]・[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]]・[[菅原道真|菅原道真公]]・不詳2座を祀る。 == 境内 == 仁田集落南部に鎮座し、付近には佐那神社に由来する「かみた」・「社はた」などの地名が残る。3,435[[坪]]（約11,355[[平方メートル|m2]]）ある[[境内]]は、標高41[[メートル|m]]の[[丘陵]]の緩やかな斜面にある式内社研究会 編（1990）：411, 413ページ。境内の南を佐奈川が流れる。境内には社務所・[[手水舎]]・参籠所・[[拝殿]]・握舎・本殿・[[神饌]]所などがある式内社研究会 編（1990）：413 - 414ページ。社務所は[[木構造 (建築)|木造]]平屋建「社務所が全焼」[[朝日新聞]]2008年6月8日付[[朝刊]]、三重版35ページで[[切妻造]]の[[瓦葺]]、本殿は南向きで[[玉垣|瑞垣]]に囲まれており、[[豊受大神宮]]（外宮）に準じた[[神明造]]である。 境内社には和玉神社と神明社がある式内社研究会 編（1990）：414ページ。郷土の国事殉難者を祀る和玉神社は拝殿・本殿・玉垣を有するが、神明社は小さな[[祠]]である。ほかに天宇受賣命の[[荒魂・和魂]]を祀る2本の自然石、大山祇神を祀る自然石、伊勢神宮と[[熊野三山|熊野三社]]の遥拝所がある。 == 信仰 == 多気町仁田・五桂の[[産土神]]である「角川日本地名大辞典」編纂委員会 編（1983）：533ページ。[[氏子]]地域は旧多気郡[[佐奈村]]全域、すなわち多気町神坂（こうざか）・五桂・五佐奈・四神田（しこうだ・しこだ）・西山・仁田・長谷・平谷・前村・[[油夫]]（ゆぶ）の10[[大字]]である式内社研究会 編（1990）：413ページ。祭神が力の神である天手力男命であることから、地元の多気町のほか、[[松阪市]]や[[亀山市]]、三重県外では[[名古屋市]]や[[大阪市]]からも参拝者が訪れる三重県立相可高校新聞部 編（2011）：1ページ。[[正月]]は特に賑わう。 == 被合祀社 == 佐那神社は以下の神社を[[1908年]]（[[明治]]41年）[[1月8日]]に合祀している式内社研究会 編（1990）：412 - 414ページ。 {| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:left; font-size:95%" |+ !'''社名'''!!'''旧所在地'''!!'''備考''' |- |八柱神社||rowspan="2"|神坂||村社 |- |浅間神社||無格社 |- |神坂八柱神社境内社2社||神坂八柱神社境内 |- |箱部神社||五桂||無格社 |- |八雲神社||rowspan="5"|五佐奈||rowspan="2"|村社 |- |八柱神社 |- |八幡神社||rowspan="3"|無格社 |- |金垣内神社 |- |澤本神社 |- |若宮神社||五佐奈八雲神社境内 |- |八柱神社||rowspan="2"|四神田||村社 |- |小社神社||無格社 |- |若宮神社||四神田八柱神社境内 |- |八雲神社||四神田小社神社境内 |- |八柱神社2社||西山||村社 |- |八雲神社||西山八柱神社境内 |- |奈良雄神社||rowspan="6"|佐奈神社境内||祭神不詳 |- |神明神社||天照大御神 |- |八雲神社||須佐之男命 |- |伊弉册神社||伊邪那美命 |- |浅間神社||木花咲耶姫命 |- |秋葉神社||火産霊神 |- |熊野三社神社||長谷||村社 |- |須麻留賣神社||rowspan="3"|平谷||rowspan="3"|村社 |- |八幡神社 |- |久保八雲神社 |- |須麻留賣神社境内社3社||須麻留賣神社境内 |- |八柱神社||rowspan="2"|前村||村社 |- |稲荷神社||無格社 |- |八柱神社||油夫||村社 |- |油夫八柱神社境内社4社||油夫八柱神社境内 |} == 歴史 == 創建は不詳であるが、『延喜式神名帳』に記載があることから、少なくとも平安時代には存在した式内社研究会 編（1990）：412ページ。[[神宮式年遷宮|伊勢神宮の式年遷宮]]に合わせて外宮造替使によって社殿が建て替えられた6社のうちの1社に名が記されている。また、[[元禄]]12年（[[1699年]]）まで有爾郷より土器が調進されていたようである。 江戸時代には、中断していた式年遷宮の制度が再開され、神宮の古材の払い下げを受けて建て替えられた。（当時の造り替え記録が佐那神社に残っている。）[[享保]]9年（[[1724年]]）、[[和歌山藩]]は当時「中の宮」などと呼ばれていたこの神社の社頭に「佐那神社」の標石を立て、式内社に比定した。氏子地域である仁田村と五桂村の村民は毎年[[正月]]に[[藁]]を納めた。[[明治4年]]（[[1871年]]）に村社に列格、[[1906年]]（明治39年）[[12月25日]]に神饌幣帛料供進社の指定を受けた。この頃、社名は「佐'''奈'''神社」となっていた。 1908年（明治41年）1月8日には佐奈村内の諸社を合祀し、同時に旧称の「佐那神社」に社名を戻した式内社研究会 編（1990）：412 - 413ページ。[[1910年]]（明治43年）1月と[[1931年]]（[[昭和]]6年）に古式に則り、式年遷宮を行った。[[1927年]]（昭和2年）11月、社殿の敷地を拡張し、[[1943年]]（昭和18年）[[10月8日]]に県社に昇格した。 [[戦後]]の[[1946年]]（昭和21年）[[5月10日]]、[[宗教法人]]となる。[[1952年]]（昭和27年）、郷土の国事殉難者の祭祀を開始し、[[1984年]]（昭和59年）11月に境内社・和玉神社として建立された。 [[2008年]]（[[平成]]20年）[[6月6日]]、佐那神社の社務所から出火、150m2を全焼した。[[2013年]]（平成25年）[[10月2日]]には60年ぶりの社殿の建て替えに向けて「[[釿|手斧]]始め祭」が行われ{{Cite web|和書|date= 2013-10-03 |url=http://yukanmie.com/blog.php?ID=6587 |title=佐那神社、工事安全祈る| publisher=[[夕刊三重]] |language=[[日本語]] |accessdate=2013-11-02|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1102-2217-00/yukanmie.com/blog.php?ID=6587|archivedate=2013-11-02}}、[[2015年]]（平成27年）[[3月13日]]に遷座祭が執り行われた{{Cite web|和書|date= 2015-03-13 |url=http://iseshima.keizai.biz/headline/2268/ |title=古事記に登場する力の神様「タジカラオ」祭る多気町・佐那神社で遷座祭| publisher=[[伊勢志摩経済新聞]] |language=日本語|accessdate=2015-04-12|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20150412082743/http://iseshima.keizai.biz/headline/2268/|archivedate=2015-04-12}}。同年[[3月29日]]には遷座を祝う「式年遷座奉祝祭」が催行された{{Cite web|和書|date= 2015-03-29 |url=http://iseshima.keizai.biz/headline/2280/ |title=樹齢350年以上志摩の一本桜「岩戸桜」ほぼ満開に―人里離れた地に人々集める| publisher=伊勢志摩経済新聞 |language=日本語 |accessdate=2015-04-12|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20150412083131/http://iseshima.keizai.biz/headline/2280/|archivedate=2015-04-12}}。 == 祭儀 == [[神職]]は[[宮司]]1名、[[禰宜]]1名と[[総代]]10名からなる。[[明治]]までは[[神主]]1名と神人6人からなる「七人衆」が社務を司り、「小野派」と「綾野派」があった。 祭儀は毎月1日と15日に行われる[[月次祭]]のほか、[[歳旦祭]]（[[1月1日]]）、[[左義長|どんど祭]]（[[1月15日]]）、[[祈年祭]]（[[3月8日]]）、[[大祓|夏越大祓]]（[[6月30日]]）、[[例祭]]（[[10月8日]]）、和玉神社慰霊祭（[[11月8日]]）、[[新嘗祭]]（[[12月8日]]）、大祓（[[12月30日]]）が行われる。例祭は[[11月5日]]だったが、1943年（昭和18年）10月8日に県社へ昇格したことを記念して10月8日に変更された。かつては村の広場にて「天鈿女踊り」（あめのうずめおどり）と呼ばれる手踊りが行われていた。 多気町長谷に[[1998年]]（平成10年）に造成された「車田」では[[もち米]]を生産しているが、毎年生産されたもち米が佐那神社に奉納されている「ぐるっと刈り取り」朝日新聞2008年9月14日付朝刊、三重版27ページ。長谷の車田御田植祭では、佐那神社の禰宜がまず[[神事]]を行う{{Cite web|和書|date= 2013 |url=http://www.ma.mctv.ne.jp/~jr2uat/18/activity.html |title=活動の成果（地域おこしグループ「一八会」）| publisher=一八会 |language=[[日本語]] |accessdate=2013-11-02|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1102-2227-01/www.ma.mctv.ne.jp/~jr2uat/18/activity.html|archivedate=2013-11-02}}。 == 交通 == 多気町立佐奈小学校の西隣にある。 ; 公共交通 * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[紀勢本線]] [[佐奈駅]]より徒歩で10分程度三重県観光連盟"[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_8476.html 佐那神社の観光施設・周辺情報 - 観光三重]"（2013年11月2日閲覧。）（北東へ約750m）。 * [[三重交通]][[路線バス|バス]]「五桂口」停留所よりすぐ。 ; 自家用車 * [[伊勢自動車道]] [[勢和多気インターチェンジ|勢和多気IC]]よる約5分。社地の南を[[国道42号]]が通っている。神社の裏手に[[駐車場]]がある"熊野古道「[[女鬼トンネル|女鬼峠]]」と伊勢路の酒蔵めぐり"朝日新聞2009年9月19日付朝刊、三重版30ページ。 == 関連書籍 == * 小野孝男『古事記 天手力男』多気ブックセンター、2014年1月24日、57p. ISBN 9784990735401{{Cite web|和書|date= 2014-03-06 |url=https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/books/R100000002-I025281074 |title= 天手力男 : 古事記 （多気ブックセンター）：2014|書誌詳細|work=国立国会図書館サーチ| publisher=[[国立国会図書館]] |language=日本語 |accessdate=2015-04-12|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20150412083131/http://iseshima.keizai.biz/headline/2280/|archivedate=2015-04-12}}{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.worldcat.org/title/amenotajikarao-kojiki/oclc/875921313 |title= 古事記 天手力男| publisher=WorldCat.org |accessdate=2017-06-03}}{{Cite web|和書|date= 2014-03-06 |url=http://iseshima.keizai.biz/headline/1977/ |title=古事記に登場する力持ちの神様「天手力男」を祭る多気町・佐那神社の本| publisher=伊勢志摩経済新聞 |language=日本語 |accessdate=2015-04-12|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20150412083131/http://iseshima.keizai.biz/headline/2280/|archivedate=2015-04-12}} - 佐奈小学校の卒業生で多気ブックセンター社長の著者が古事記や佐那神社、同社の祭神である天手力男について知ってもらうために出版した書籍。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 「角川日本地名大辞典」編纂委員会 編『角川日本地名大辞典 24三重県』[[角川書店]]、昭和58年6月8日、1643p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[三重県立相可高等学校|三重県立相可高校]]新聞部 編『多気ぴぃ vol.1』平成23年2月1日、多気Pプロジェクト、2p. * 『三重県の地名』日本歴史地名大系24、[[平凡社]]、1983年5月20日、1081p. == 関連項目 == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 佐那神社] - 三重県神社庁 {{デフォルトソート:さなしんしや}} [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:多気町の歴史]] [[Category:2015年竣工の日本の建築物]] [[Category:多気町の建築物]]

{{Shrine | name = Sana Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sana Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location = [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki]], [[Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], Nita 156 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|51.3|N|136|32|46.1|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Enshrined Deities = [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]]
[[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] | type = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha (Minor)]], Former Prefectural Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = [[Shimmei-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Nakamiya, Ōmori Shrine | festival = [[October 8]] | Rituals = }} '''Sana Shrine''' (佐那神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki District]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine has historical ties to the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] and is associated with [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]], who is also enshrined as a [[secondary deity]] at the Ise Grand Shrine. == name and Etymology == The name "Sana" originates from the place name "Sana-no-agata" mentioned in ancient texts like the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Kotaijingu Gishikicho]]''.''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 411. In the [[Edo period]], the shrine was popularly called "Ōmori Shrine" or "Nakamiya." The name "Nakamiya" is believed to relate to its status as one of three prominent shrines in the region, alongside Sumaru-me Shrine (Ichinomiya) and [[Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)|Kushida Shrine]] (Taisha). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the following two deities: * [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]] * [[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] Ame-no-Tajikarao is mentioned in the ''Kojiki'' as residing in "Sana-no-agata," while Akebono-Tatsu-O is described as the ancestor of the [[Sana clan]]. Additional deities from merged shrines include: * [[Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]] * [[Izanami-no-Mikoto]] * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Susanoo]] * [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] * [[Ame-no-Hohi]] * [[Amatsuhikone]] * [[Ikutsuhikone]] * [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano-Kusubi]] * [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Tagorihime]] * [[Ichikishimahime]] * [[Kagutsuchi]] * [[Ukanomitama]] * [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]] * [[Konohanasakuya-hime]] * [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Koto-shiro-nushi]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927) as a minor shrine (小社).''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 412. The shrine's main hall has historically been rebuilt using materials donated from the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] during its [[Shikinen Sengu|Shikinen Sengu]] reconstruction. In the Edo period, the shrine received official recognition as a ''Shikinaisha'' and was referred to as "Sana Shrine" on a stone marker erected in [[1724]]. It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in [[1943]]. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from [[Matsusaka]], [[Kameyama]], and as far as [[Nagoya]] and [[Osaka]].Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[JR Central|JR]] [[Kisei Main Line]] [[Sana Station]], approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. It is also accessible via [[Mie Kotsu]] bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Taki, Mie]] * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en}}[[ja:佐那神社]] ({{ill|Taki District|en}}[[ja:多気郡]], {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}[[ja:三重県]]) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en}}[[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en}}[[ja:尼崎市]], {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en}}[[ja:兵庫県]]) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en}}[[ja:雄山神社]] ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en}}[[ja:立山町]], {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en}}[[ja:上新川郡]], {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en}}[[ja:富山県]]) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Tomei Shrine|en}}[[ja:戸明神社]] ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en}}[[ja:北九州市]], {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en}}[[ja:福岡県]]) disambiguation]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sana Shrine}}[[Tajikarao Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines by Deity]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sana Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tajikarao Shrine]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sana Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:refresh]] [[Category:Tajikarao Shrine]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sana Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[ja:手力雄神社]]

{{Shrine | name = Sana Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sana Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location = [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki]], [[Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], Nita 156 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|51.3|N|136|32|46.1|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Enshrined Deities = [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]]
[[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] | type = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha (Minor)]], Former Prefectural Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = [[Shimmei-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Nakamiya, Ōmori Shrine | festival = [[October 8]] | Rituals = }} '''Sana Shrine''' (佐那神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki District]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine has historical ties to the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] and is associated with [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]], who is also enshrined as a [[secondary deity]] at the Ise Grand Shrine. == name and Etymology == The name "Sana" originates from the place name "Sana-no-agata" mentioned in ancient texts like the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Kotaijingu Gishikicho]]''.''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 411. In the [[Edo period]], the shrine was popularly called "Ōmori Shrine" or "Nakamiya." The name "Nakamiya" is believed to relate to its status as one of three prominent shrines in the region, alongside Sumaru-me Shrine (Ichinomiya) and [[Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)|Kushida Shrine]] (Taisha). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the following two deities: * [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]] * [[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] Ame-no-Tajikarao is mentioned in the ''Kojiki'' as residing in "Sana-no-agata," while Akebono-Tatsu-O is described as the ancestor of the [[Sana clan]]. Additional deities from merged shrines include: * [[Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]] * [[Izanami-no-Mikoto]] * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Susanoo]] * [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] * [[Ame-no-Hohi]] * [[Amatsuhikone]] * [[Ikutsuhikone]] * [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano-Kusubi]] * [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Tagorihime]] * [[Ichikishimahime]] * [[Kagutsuchi]] * [[Ukanomitama]] * [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]] * [[Konohanasakuya-hime]] * [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Koto-shiro-nushi]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927) as a minor shrine (小社).''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 412. The shrine's main hall has historically been rebuilt using materials donated from the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] during its [[Shikinen Sengu|Shikinen Sengu]] reconstruction. In the Edo period, the shrine received official recognition as a ''Shikinaisha'' and was referred to as "Sana Shrine" on a stone marker erected in [[1724]]. It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in [[1943]]. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from [[Matsusaka]], [[Kameyama]], and as far as [[Nagoya]] and [[Osaka]].Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[JR Central|JR]] [[Kisei Main Line]] [[Sana Station]], approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. It is also accessible via [[Mie Kotsu]] bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Taki, Mie]] * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en}}[[ja:佐那神社]] ({{ill|Taki District|en}}[[ja:多気郡]], {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}[[ja:三重県]]) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en}}[[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en}}[[ja:尼崎市]], {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en}}[[ja:兵庫県]]) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en}}[[ja:雄山神社]] ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en}}[[ja:立山町]], {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en}}[[ja:上新川郡]], {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en}}[[ja:富山県]]) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Tomei Shrine|en}}[[ja:戸明神社]] ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en}}[[ja:北九州市]], {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en}}[[ja:福岡県]]) disambiguation]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sana Shrine}}[[Tajikarao Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines by Deity]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sana Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tajikarao Shrine]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Sana Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sana Shrine]][[Category:refresh]] [[Category:Tajikarao Shrine]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sana Shrine]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Sana Shrine]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[ja:手力雄神社]]

{{Shrine | name = Sana Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sana Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location = [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki]], [[Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], Nita 156 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|51.3|N|136|32|46.1|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Enshrined Deities = [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]]
[[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] | type = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha (Minor)]], Former Prefectural Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = [[Shimmei-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Nakamiya, Ōmori Shrine | festival = [[October 8]] | Rituals = }} '''Sana Shrine''' (佐那神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki District]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine has historical ties to the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] and is associated with [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]], who is also enshrined as a [[secondary deity]] at the Ise Grand Shrine. == name and Etymology == The name "Sana" originates from the place name "Sana-no-agata" mentioned in ancient texts like the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Kotaijingu Gishikicho]]''.''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 411. In the [[Edo period]], the shrine was popularly called "Ōmori Shrine" or "Nakamiya." The name "Nakamiya" is believed to relate to its status as one of three prominent shrines in the region, alongside Sumaru-me Shrine (Ichinomiya) and [[Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)|Kushida Shrine]] (Taisha). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the following two deities: * [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]] * [[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] Ame-no-Tajikarao is mentioned in the ''Kojiki'' as residing in "Sana-no-agata," while Akebono-Tatsu-O is described as the ancestor of the [[Sana clan]]. Additional deities from merged shrines include: * [[Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]] * [[Izanami-no-Mikoto]] * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Susanoo]] * [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] * [[Ame-no-Hohi]] * [[Amatsuhikone]] * [[Ikutsuhikone]] * [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano-Kusubi]] * [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Tagorihime]] * [[Ichikishimahime]] * [[Kagutsuchi]] * [[Ukanomitama]] * [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]] * [[Konohanasakuya-hime]] * [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Koto-shiro-nushi]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927) as a minor shrine (小社).''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 412. The shrine's main hall has historically been rebuilt using materials donated from the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] during its [[Shikinen Sengu|Shikinen Sengu]] reconstruction. In the Edo period, the shrine received official recognition as a ''Shikinaisha'' and was referred to as "Sana Shrine" on a stone marker erected in [[1724]]. It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in [[1943]]. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from [[Matsusaka]], [[Kameyama]], and as far as [[Nagoya]] and [[Osaka]].Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[JR Central|JR]] [[Kisei Main Line]] [[Sana Station]], approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. It is also accessible via [[Mie Kotsu]] bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Taki, Mie]] * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en}}[[ja:佐那神社]] ({{ill|Taki District|en}}[[ja:多気郡]], {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}[[ja:三重県]]) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en}}[[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en}}[[ja:尼崎市]], {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en}}[[ja:兵庫県]]) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en}}[[ja:雄山神社]] ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en}}[[ja:立山町]], {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en}}[[ja:上新川郡]], {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en}}[[ja:富山県]]) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Tomei Shrine|en}}[[ja:戸明神社]] ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en}}[[ja:北九州市]], {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en}}[[ja:福岡県]]) disambiguation]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sana Shrine}}[[Tajikarao Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines by deity]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sana Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tajikarao Shrine]][[Category:Sana Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Sana Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Sana Shrine]][[Category:refresh]] [[Category:Tajikarao Shrine]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Sana Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Sana Shrine]][[Category:Sana Shrine]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[ja:手力雄神社]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Sana Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sana Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location = [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki]], [[Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], Nita 156 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|51.3|N|136|32|46.1|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Enshrined Deities = [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]]
[[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] | type = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha (Minor)]], Former Prefectural Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = [[Shimmei-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Nakamiya, Ōmori Shrine | festival = [[October 8]] | Rituals = }} '''Sana Shrine''' (佐那神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki District]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine has historical ties to the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] and is associated with [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]], who is also enshrined as a [[secondary deity]] at the Ise Grand Shrine. == name and Etymology == The name "Sana" originates from the place name "Sana-no-agata" mentioned in ancient texts like the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Kotaijingu Gishikicho]]''.''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 411. In the [[Edo period]], the shrine was popularly called "Ōmori Shrine" or "Nakamiya." The name "Nakamiya" is believed to relate to its status as one of three prominent shrines in the region, alongside Sumaru-me Shrine (Ichinomiya) and [[Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)|Kushida Shrine]] (Taisha). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the following two deities: * [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]] * [[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] Ame-no-Tajikarao is mentioned in the ''Kojiki'' as residing in "Sana-no-agata," while Akebono-Tatsu-O is described as the ancestor of the [[Sana clan]]. Additional deities from merged shrines include: * [[Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]] * [[Izanami-no-Mikoto]] * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Susanoo]] * [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] * [[Ame-no-Hohi]] * [[Amatsuhikone]] * [[Ikutsuhikone]] * [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano-Kusubi]] * [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Tagorihime]] * [[Ichikishimahime]] * [[Kagutsuchi]] * [[Ukanomitama]] * [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]] * [[Konohanasakuya-hime]] * [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Koto-shiro-nushi]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927) as a minor shrine (小社).''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 412. The shrine's main hall has historically been rebuilt using materials donated from the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] during its [[Shikinen Sengu|Shikinen Sengu]] reconstruction. In the Edo period, the shrine received official recognition as a ''Shikinaisha'' and was referred to as "Sana Shrine" on a stone marker erected in [[1724]]. It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in [[1943]]. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from [[Matsusaka]], [[Kameyama]], and as far as [[Nagoya]] and [[Osaka]].Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[JR Central|JR]] [[Kisei Main Line]] [[Sana Station]], approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. It is also accessible via [[Mie Kotsu]] bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Taki, Mie]] * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en}}[[ja:佐那神社]] ({{ill|Taki District|en}}[[ja:多気郡]], {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}[[ja:三重県]]) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en}}[[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en}}[[ja:尼崎市]], {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en}}[[ja:兵庫県]]) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en}}[[ja:雄山神社]] ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en}}[[ja:立山町]], {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en}}[[ja:上新川郡]], {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en}}[[ja:富山県]]) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Tomei Shrine|en}}[[ja:戸明神社]] ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en}}[[ja:北九州市]], {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en}}[[ja:福岡県]]) disambiguation]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sana Shrine}}[[Tajikarao Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines by deity]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sana Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tajikarao Shrine]][[Category:Sana Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sana Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Sana Shrine]][[Category:refresh]] [[Category:Tajikarao Shrine]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sana Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Sana Shrine]][[Category:Sana Shrine]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Sana Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sana Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sana Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sana Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:手力雄神社]]

It is also accessible via [[Mie Kotsu]] bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Taki, Mie]] * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en}}[[ja:佐那神社]] ({{ill|Taki District|en}}[[ja:多気郡]], {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}[[ja:三重県]]) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en}}[[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en}}[[ja:尼崎市]], {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en}}[[ja:兵庫県]]) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en}}[[ja:雄山神社]] ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en}}[[ja:立山町]], {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en}}[[ja:上新川郡]], {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en}}[[ja:富山県]]) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Tomei Shrine|en}}[[ja:戸明神社]] ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en}}[[ja:北九州市]], {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en}}[[ja:福岡県]]) disambiguation]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sana Shrine}}[[Tajikarao Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines by deity]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sana Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tajikarao Shrine]][[Category:Sana Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sana Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Sana Shrine]][[Category:refresh]] [[Category:Tajikarao Shrine]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sana Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Sana Shrine]][[Category:Sana Shrine]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Sana Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sana Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sana Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sana Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sana Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sana Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sana Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sana Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:手力雄神社]]

{{Shrine | name = Sana Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sana Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location = [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki]], [[Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], Nita 156 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|51.3|N|136|32|46.1|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Enshrined Deities = [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]]
[[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] | type = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha (Minor)]], Former Prefectural Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = [[Shimmei-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Nakamiya, Ōmori Shrine | festival = [[October 8]] | Rituals = }} '''Sana Shrine''' (佐那神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki District]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine has historical ties to the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] and is associated with [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]], who is also enshrined as a [[secondary deity]] at the Ise Grand Shrine. == name and Etymology == The name "Sana" originates from the place name "Sana-no-agata" mentioned in ancient texts like the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Kotaijingu Gishikicho]]''.''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 411. In the [[Edo period]], the shrine was popularly called "Ōmori Shrine" or "Nakamiya." The name "Nakamiya" is believed to relate to its status as one of three prominent shrines in the region, alongside Sumaru-me Shrine (Ichinomiya) and [[Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)|Kushida Shrine]] (Taisha). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the following two deities: * [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]] * [[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] Ame-no-Tajikarao is mentioned in the ''Kojiki'' as residing in "Sana-no-agata," while Akebono-Tatsu-O is described as the ancestor of the [[Sana clan]]. Additional deities from merged shrines include: * [[Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]] * [[Izanami-no-Mikoto]] * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Susanoo]] * [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] * [[Ame-no-Hohi]] * [[Amatsuhikone]] * [[Ikutsuhikone]] * [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano-Kusubi]] * [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Tagorihime]] * [[Ichikishimahime]] * [[Kagutsuchi]] * [[Ukanomitama]] * [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]] * [[Konohanasakuya-hime]] * [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Koto-shiro-nushi]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927) as a minor shrine (小社).''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 412. The shrine's main hall has historically been rebuilt using materials donated from the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] during its [[Shikinen Sengu|Shikinen Sengu]] reconstruction. In the Edo period, the shrine received official recognition as a ''Shikinaisha'' and was referred to as "Sana Shrine" on a stone marker erected in [[1724]]. It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in [[1943]]. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from [[Matsusaka]], [[Kameyama]], and as far as [[Nagoya]] and [[Osaka]].Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[JR Central|JR]] [[Kisei Main Line]] [[Sana Station]], approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. It is also accessible via [[Mie Kotsu]] bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Taki, Mie]] * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en}}[[ja:佐那神社]] ({{ill|Taki District|en}}[[ja:多気郡]], {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}[[ja:三重県]]) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en}}[[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en}}[[ja:尼崎市]], {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en}}[[ja:兵庫県]]) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en}}[[ja:雄山神社]] ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en}}[[ja:立山町]], {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en}}[[ja:上新川郡]], {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en}}[[ja:富山県]]) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Tomei Shrine|en}}[[ja:戸明神社]] ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en}}[[ja:北九州市]], {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en}}[[ja:福岡県]]) disambiguation]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sana Shrine}}[[Tajikarao Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines by deity]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sana Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tajikarao Shrine]][[Category:Sana Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sana Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Sana Shrine]][[Category:refresh]] [[Category:Tajikarao Shrine]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sana Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Sana Shrine]][[Category:Sana Shrine]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Sana Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sana Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sana Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sana Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sana Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sana Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sana Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sana Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Sana Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sana Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sana Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sana Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sana Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:手力雄神社]]

[[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] | type = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha (Minor)]], Former Prefectural Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = [[Shimmei-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Nakamiya, Ōmori Shrine | festival = [[October 8]] | Rituals = }} '''Sana Shrine''' (佐那神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki District]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine has historical ties to the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] and is associated with [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]], who is also enshrined as a [[secondary deity]] at the Ise Grand Shrine. == name and Etymology == The name "Sana" originates from the place name "Sana-no-agata" mentioned in ancient texts like the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Kotaijingu Gishikicho]]''.''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 411. In the [[Edo period]], the shrine was popularly called "Ōmori Shrine" or "Nakamiya." The name "Nakamiya" is believed to relate to its status as one of three prominent shrines in the region, alongside Sumaru-me Shrine (Ichinomiya) and [[Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)|Kushida Shrine]] (Taisha). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the following two deities: * [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]] * [[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] Ame-no-Tajikarao is mentioned in the ''Kojiki'' as residing in "Sana-no-agata," while Akebono-Tatsu-O is described as the ancestor of the [[Sana clan]]. Additional deities from merged shrines include: * [[Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]] * [[Izanami-no-Mikoto]] * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Susanoo]] * [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] * [[Ame-no-Hohi]] * [[Amatsuhikone]] * [[Ikutsuhikone]] * [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano-Kusubi]] * [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Tagorihime]] * [[Ichikishimahime]] * [[Kagutsuchi]] * [[Ukanomitama]] * [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]] * [[Konohanasakuya-hime]] * [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Koto-shiro-nushi]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927) as a minor shrine (小社).''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 412. The shrine's main hall has historically been rebuilt using materials donated from the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] during its [[Shikinen Sengu|Shikinen Sengu]] reconstruction. In the Edo period, the shrine received official recognition as a ''Shikinaisha'' and was referred to as "Sana Shrine" on a stone marker erected in [[1724]]. It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in [[1943]]. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from [[Matsusaka]], [[Kameyama]], and as far as [[Nagoya]] and [[Osaka]].Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[JR Central|JR]] [[Kisei Main Line]] [[Sana Station]], approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. It is also accessible via [[Mie Kotsu]] bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Taki, Mie]] * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en}}[[ja:佐那神社]] ({{ill|Taki District|en}}[[ja:多気郡]], {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}[[ja:三重県]]) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en}}[[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en}}[[ja:尼崎市]], {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en}}[[ja:兵庫県]]) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en}}[[ja:雄山神社]] ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en}}[[ja:立山町]], {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en}}[[ja:上新川郡]], {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en}}[[ja:富山県]]) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Tomei Shrine|en}}[[ja:戸明神社]] ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en}}[[ja:北九州市]], {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en}}[[ja:福岡県]]) disambiguation]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sana Shrine}}[[Tajikarao Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines by deity]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sana Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tajikarao Shrine]][[Category:Sana Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sana Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Sana Shrine]][[Category:refresh]] [[Category:Tajikarao Shrine]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sana Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Sana Shrine]][[Category:Sana Shrine]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Sana Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sana Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sana Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sana Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sana Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sana Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sana Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sana Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Sana Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sana Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sana Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sana Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sana Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:手力雄神社]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sana Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

It is also accessible via [[Mie Kotsu]] bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - {{Shrine | name = Sana Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sana Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location = [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki]], [[Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], Nita 156 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|51.3|N|136|32|46.1|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Enshrined Deities = [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]]
[[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] | type = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha (Minor)]], Former Prefectural Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = [[Shimmei-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Nakamiya, Ōmori Shrine | festival = [[October 8]] | Rituals = }} '''Sana Shrine''' (佐那神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki District]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine has historical ties to the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] and is associated with [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]], who is also enshrined as a [[secondary deity]] at the Ise Grand Shrine. == name and Etymology == The name "Sana" originates from the place name "Sana-no-agata" mentioned in ancient texts like the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Kotaijingu Gishikicho]]''.''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 411. In the [[Edo period]], the shrine was popularly called "Ōmori Shrine" or "Nakamiya." The name "Nakamiya" is believed to relate to its status as one of three prominent shrines in the region, alongside Sumaru-me Shrine (Ichinomiya) and [[Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)|Kushida Shrine]] (Taisha). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the following two deities: * [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]] * [[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] Ame-no-Tajikarao is mentioned in the ''Kojiki'' as residing in "Sana-no-agata," while Akebono-Tatsu-O is described as the ancestor of the [[Sana clan]]. Additional deities from merged shrines include: * [[Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]] * [[Izanami-no-Mikoto]] * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Susanoo]] * [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] * [[Ame-no-Hohi]] * [[Amatsuhikone]] * [[Ikutsuhikone]] * [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano-Kusubi]] * [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Tagorihime]] * [[Ichikishimahime]] * [[Kagutsuchi]] * [[Ukanomitama]] * [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]] * [[Konohanasakuya-hime]] * [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Koto-shiro-nushi]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927) as a minor shrine (小社).''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 412. The shrine's main hall has historically been rebuilt using materials donated from the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] during its [[Shikinen Sengu|Shikinen Sengu]] reconstruction. In the Edo period, the shrine received official recognition as a ''Shikinaisha'' and was referred to as "Sana Shrine" on a stone marker erected in [[1724]]. It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in [[1943]]. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from [[Matsusaka]], [[Kameyama]], and as far as [[Nagoya]] and [[Osaka]].Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[JR Central|JR]] [[Kisei Main Line]] [[Sana Station]], approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. It is also accessible via [[Mie Kotsu]] bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie {{Shrine | name = Sana Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sana Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location = [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki]], [[Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], Nita 156 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|51.3|N|136|32|46.1|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Enshrined Deities = [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]]
[[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] | type = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha (Minor)]], Former Prefectural Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = [[Shimmei-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Nakamiya, Ōmori Shrine | festival = [[October 8]] | Rituals = }} '''Sana Shrine''' (佐那神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki District]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine has historical ties to the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] and is associated with [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]], who is also enshrined as a [[secondary deity]] at the Ise Grand Shrine. == name and Etymology == The name "Sana" originates from the place name "Sana-no-agata" mentioned in ancient texts like the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Kotaijingu Gishikicho]]''.''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 411. In the [[Edo period]], the shrine was popularly called "Ōmori Shrine" or "Nakamiya." The name "Nakamiya" is believed to relate to its status as one of three prominent shrines in the region, alongside Sumaru-me Shrine (Ichinomiya) and [[Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)|Kushida Shrine]] (Taisha). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the following two deities: * [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]] * [[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] Ame-no-Tajikarao is mentioned in the ''Kojiki'' as residing in "Sana-no-agata," while Akebono-Tatsu-O is described as the ancestor of the [[Sana clan]]. Additional deities from merged shrines include: * [[Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]] * [[Izanami-no-Mikoto]] * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Susanoo]] * [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] * [[Ame-no-Hohi]] * [[Amatsuhikone]] * [[Ikutsuhikone]] * [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano-Kusubi]] * [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Tagorihime]] * [[Ichikishimahime]] * [[Kagutsuchi]] * [[Ukanomitama]] * [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]] * [[Konohanasakuya-hime]] * [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Koto-shiro-nushi]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927) as a minor shrine (小社).''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 412. The shrine's main hall has historically been rebuilt using materials donated from the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] during its [[Shikinen Sengu|Shikinen Sengu]] reconstruction. In the Edo period, the shrine received official recognition as a ''Shikinaisha'' and was referred to as "Sana Shrine" on a stone marker erected in [[1724]]. It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in [[1943]]. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from [[Matsusaka]], [[Kameyama]], and as far as [[Nagoya]] and [[Osaka]].Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[JR Central|JR]] [[Kisei Main Line]] [[Sana Station]], approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. It is also accessible via [[Mie Kotsu]] bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Taki, Mie]] * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en}}[[ja:佐那神社]] ({{ill|Taki District|en}}[[ja:多気郡]], {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}[[ja:三重県]]) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en}}[[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en}}[[ja:尼崎市]], {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en}}[[ja:兵庫県]]) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en}}[[ja:雄山神社]] ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en}}[[ja:立山町]], {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en}}[[ja:上新川郡]], {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en}}[[ja:富山県]]) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Tomei Shrine|en}}[[ja:戸明神社]] ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en}}[[ja:北九州市]], {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en}}[[ja:福岡県]]) disambiguation]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sana Shrine}}[[Tajikarao Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines by deity]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sana Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tajikarao Shrine]][[Category:Sana Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sana Shrine-qq]] [[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] | type = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha (Minor)]], Former Prefectural Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = [[Shimmei-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Nakamiya, Ōmori Shrine | festival = [[October 8]] | Rituals = }} '''Sana Shrine''' (佐那神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki District]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine has historical ties to the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] and is associated with [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]], who is also enshrined as a [[secondary deity]] at the Ise Grand Shrine. == name and Etymology == The name "Sana" originates from the place name "Sana-no-agata" mentioned in ancient texts like the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Kotaijingu Gishikicho]]''.''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 411. In the [[Edo period]], the shrine was popularly called "Ōmori Shrine" or "Nakamiya." The name "Nakamiya" is believed to relate to its status as one of three prominent shrines in the region, alongside Sumaru-me Shrine (Ichinomiya) and [[Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)|Kushida Shrine]] (Taisha). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the following two deities: * [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]] * [[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] Ame-no-Tajikarao is mentioned in the ''Kojiki'' as residing in "Sana-no-agata," while Akebono-Tatsu-O is described as the ancestor of the [[Sana clan]]. The shrine's main hall has historically been rebuilt using materials donated from the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] during its [[Shikinen Sengu|Shikinen Sengu]] reconstruction. In the Edo period, the shrine received official recognition as a ''Shikinaisha'' and was referred to as "Sana Shrine" on a stone marker erected in [[1724]]. It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in [[1943]]. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from [[Matsusaka]], [[Kameyama]], and as far as [[Nagoya]] and [[Osaka]].Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[JR Central|JR]] [[Kisei Main Line]] [[Sana Station]], approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. It is also accessible via [[Mie Kotsu]] bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * The shrine's main hall has historically been rebuilt using materials donated from the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] during its [[Shikinen Sengu|Shikinen Sengu]] reconstruction. In the Edo period, the shrine received official recognition as a ''Shikinaisha'' and was referred to as "Sana Shrine" on a stone marker erected in [[1724]]. It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in [[1943]]. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from [[Matsusaka]], [[Kameyama]], and as far as [[Nagoya]] and [[Osaka]].Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[JR Central|JR]] [[Kisei Main Line]] [[Sana Station]], approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. It is also accessible via [[Mie Kotsu]] bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Shrine | name = Sana Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sana Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location = [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki]], [[Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], Nita 156 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|51.3|N|136|32|46.1|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Enshrined Deities = [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]]
[[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] | type = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha (Minor)]], Former Prefectural Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = [[Shimmei-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Nakamiya, Ōmori Shrine | festival = [[October 8]] | Rituals = }} '''Sana Shrine''' (佐那神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki District]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine has historical ties to the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] and is associated with [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]], who is also enshrined as a [[secondary deity]] at the Ise Grand Shrine. == name and Etymology == The name "Sana" originates from the place name "Sana-no-agata" mentioned in ancient texts like the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Kotaijingu Gishikicho]]''.''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 411. In the [[Edo period]], the shrine was popularly called "Ōmori Shrine" or "Nakamiya." The name "Nakamiya" is believed to relate to its status as one of three prominent shrines in the region, alongside Sumaru-me Shrine (Ichinomiya) and [[Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)|Kushida Shrine]] (Taisha). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the following two deities: * [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]] * [[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] Ame-no-Tajikarao is mentioned in the ''Kojiki'' as residing in "Sana-no-agata," while Akebono-Tatsu-O is described as the ancestor of the [[Sana clan]]. Additional deities from merged shrines include: * [[Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[Hayatama-no-Mikoto|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]] * [[Izanami-no-Mikoto]] * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Susanoo]] * [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] * [[Ame-no-Hohi]] * [[Amatsuhikone]] * [[Ikutsuhikone]] * [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano-Kusubi]] * [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Tagorihime]] * [[Ichikishimahime]] * [[Kagutsuchi]] * [[Ukanomitama]] * [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]] * [[Konohanasakuya-hime]] * [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Koto-shiro-nushi]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927) as a minor shrine (小社).''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 412. The shrine's main hall has historically been rebuilt using materials donated from the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] during its [[Shikinen Sengu|Shikinen Sengu]] reconstruction. In the Edo period, the shrine received official recognition as a ''Shikinaisha'' and was referred to as "Sana Shrine" on a stone marker erected in [[1724]]. It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in [[1943]]. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from [[Matsusaka]], [[Kameyama]], and as far as [[Nagoya]] and [[Osaka]].Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[JR Central|JR]] [[Kisei Main Line]] [[Sana Station]], approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. It is also accessible via [[Mie Kotsu]] bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Taki, Mie]] * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en}}[[ja:佐那神社]] ({{ill|Taki District|en}}[[ja:多気郡]], {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}[[ja:三重県]]) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en}}[[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en}}[[ja:尼崎市]], {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en}}[[ja:兵庫県]]) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en}}[[ja:雄山神社]] ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en}}[[ja:立山町]], {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en}}[[ja:上新川郡]], {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en}}[[ja:富山県]]) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Tomei Shrine|en}}[[ja:戸明神社]] ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en}}[[ja:北九州市]], {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en}}[[ja:福岡県]]) disambiguation]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sana Shrine}}[[Tajikarao Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines
