The name "Nakamiya" is believed to relate to its status as one of three prominent shrines in the region, alongside Sumaru-me Shrine (Ichinomiya) and [[Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)|Kushida Shrine]] (Taisha). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the following two deities: * [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]] * [[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] Ame-no-Tajikarao is mentioned in the ''Kojiki'' as residing in "Sana-no-agata," while Akebono-Tatsu-O is described as the ancestor of the [[Sana clan]]. Additional deities from merged shrines include: * [[Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[Kumano Gongen|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]] * [[Izanami-no-Mikoto]] * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Susanoo]] * [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] * [[Ame-no-Hohi]] * [[Amatsuhikone]] * [[Ikutsuhikone]] * [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano-Kusubi]] * [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Tagorihime]] * [[Ichikishima-hime]] * [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] * [[Ukanomitama]] * [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]] * [[Konohanasakuya-hime]] * [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Koto-shiro-nushi]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927) as a minor shrine (小社).''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 412. The shrine's main hall has historically been rebuilt using materials donated from the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] during its [[Jingū Shikinen Sengū|Shikinen Sengu]] reconstruction. In the Edo period, the shrine received official recognition as a ''Shikinaisha'' and was referred to as "Sana Shrine" on a stone marker erected in [[1724]]. It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in [[1943]]. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from [[Matsusaka]], [[Kameyama]], and as far as [[Nagoya]] and [[Osaka]].Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[JR Central|JR]] [[Kisei Main Line]] [[Sana Station]], approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. It is also accessible via [[Mie Kotsu]] bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sana Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Taki District|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki District|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|qq=}} ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagasaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakaniikawa District|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefecture|qq=}}) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Togami Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomei Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakyūshū City|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}) disambiguation]] [[Tajikarao Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|佐那神社|version=103551216|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sana Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tajikarao Shrine]] [[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:多気郡]] [[ja:三重県]] [[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] [[ja:尼崎市]] [[ja:兵庫県]] [[ja:雄山神社]] [[ja:立山町]] [[ja:上新川郡]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:北九州市]] [[ja:福岡県]] [[sn:Sana Shrine]] [[ja:手力雄神社]]

It is also accessible via [[Mie Kotsu]] bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sana Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Taki District|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki District|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|qq=}} ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagasaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakaniikawa District|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefecture|qq=}}) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Togami Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomei Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakyūshū City|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}) disambiguation]] [[Tajikarao Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|佐那神社|version=103551216|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sana Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tajikarao Shrine]] [[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:多気郡]] [[ja:三重県]] [[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] [[ja:尼崎市]] [[ja:兵庫県]] [[ja:雄山神社]] [[ja:立山町]] [[ja:上新川郡]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:北九州市]] [[ja:福岡県]] [[sn:Sana Shrine]] [[ja:手力雄神社]] [[Category:2015年竣工の日本の建築物]]

[[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] | type = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha (Minor)]], Former Prefectural Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = [[Shimmei-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Nakamiya, Ōmori Shrine | festival = [[October 8]] | Rituals = }} '''Sana Shrine''' (佐那神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki District]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine has historical ties to the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] and is associated with [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]], who is also enshrined as a [[secondary deity]] at the Ise Grand Shrine. == name and Etymology == The name "Sana" originates from the place name "Sana-no-agata" mentioned in ancient texts like the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Kotaijingu Gishikicho]]''.''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 411. In the [[Edo period]], the shrine was popularly called "Ōmori Shrine" or "Nakamiya." The name "Nakamiya" is believed to relate to its status as one of three prominent shrines in the region, alongside Sumaru-me Shrine (Ichinomiya) and [[Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)|Kushida Shrine]] (Taisha). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the following two deities: * [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]] * [[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] Ame-no-Tajikarao is mentioned in the ''Kojiki'' as residing in "Sana-no-agata," while Akebono-Tatsu-O is described as the ancestor of the [[Sana clan]]. Additional deities from merged shrines include: * [[Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[Kumano Gongen|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]] * [[Izanami-no-Mikoto]] * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] * [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] * [[Ame-no-Hohi]] * [[Amatsuhikone]] * [[Ikutsuhikone]] * [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano-Kusubi]] * [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Tagorihime]] * [[Ichikishima-hime]] * [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] * [[Ukanomitama]] * [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]] * [[Konohanasakuya-hime]] * [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Koto-shiro-nushi]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927) as a minor shrine (小社).''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 412. The shrine's main hall has historically been rebuilt using materials donated from the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] during its [[Jingū Shikinen Sengū|Shikinen Sengu]] reconstruction. In the Edo period, the shrine received official recognition as a ''Shikinaisha'' and was referred to as "Sana Shrine" on a stone marker erected in [[1724]]. It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in [[1943]]. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from [[Matsusaka]], [[Kameyama]], and as far as [[Nagoya]] and [[Osaka]].Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[JR Central|JR]] [[Kisei Main Line]] [[Sana Station]], approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. It is also accessible via [[Mie Kotsu]] bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sana Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Taki District|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki District|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|qq=}} ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagasaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakaniikawa District|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefecture|qq=}}) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Togami Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomei Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakyūshū City|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}) disambiguation]] [[Tajikarao Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|佐那神社|version=103551216|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sana Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tajikarao Shrine]] [[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:多気郡]] [[ja:三重県]] [[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] [[ja:尼崎市]] [[ja:兵庫県]] [[ja:雄山神社]] [[ja:立山町]] [[ja:上新川郡]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:北九州市]] [[ja:福岡県]] [[sn:Sana Shrine]] [[ja:手力雄神社]] [[Category:2015年竣工の日本の建築物]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]]

The shrine has historical ties to the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] and is associated with [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]], who is also enshrined as a [[secondary deity]] at the Ise Grand Shrine. == name and Etymology == The name "Sana" originates from the place name "Sana-no-agata" mentioned in ancient texts like the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Kotaijingu Gishikicho]]''.''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 411. In the [[Edo period]], the shrine was popularly called "Ōmori Shrine" or "Nakamiya." The name "Nakamiya" is believed to relate to its status as one of three prominent shrines in the region, alongside Sumaru-me Shrine (Ichinomiya) and [[Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)|Kushida Shrine]] (Taisha). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the following two deities: * [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]] * [[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] Ame-no-Tajikarao is mentioned in the ''Kojiki'' as residing in "Sana-no-agata," while Akebono-Tatsu-O is described as the ancestor of the [[Sana clan]]. Additional deities from merged shrines include: * [[Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[Kumano Gongen|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]] * [[Izanami-no-Mikoto]] * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] * [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] * [[Ame-no-Hohi]] * [[Amatsuhikone]] * [[Ikutsuhikone]] * [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano-Kusubi]] * [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Tagorihime]] * [[Ichikishima-hime]] * [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] * [[Ukanomitama]] * [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]] * [[Konohanasakuya-hime]] * [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Koto-shiro-nushi]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927) as a minor shrine (小社).''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 412. The shrine's main hall has historically been rebuilt using materials donated from the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] during its [[Jingū Shikinen Sengū|Shikinen Sengu]] reconstruction. In the Edo period, the shrine received official recognition as a ''Shikinaisha'' and was referred to as "Sana Shrine" on a stone marker erected in [[1724]]. It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in [[1943]]. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from [[Matsusaka]], [[Kameyama]], and as far as [[Nagoya]] and [[Osaka]].Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[JR Central|JR]] [[Kisei Main Line]] [[Sana Station]], approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. It is also accessible via [[Mie Kotsu]] bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sana Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Taki District|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki District|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|qq=}} ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagasaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakaniikawa District|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefecture|qq=}}) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Togami Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomei Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakyūshū City|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}) disambiguation]] [[Tajikarao Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|佐那神社|version=103551216|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sana Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tajikarao Shrine]] [[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:多気郡]] [[ja:三重県]] [[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] [[ja:尼崎市]] [[ja:兵庫県]] [[ja:雄山神社]] [[ja:立山町]] [[ja:上新川郡]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:北九州市]] [[ja:福岡県]] [[sn:Sana Shrine]] [[ja:手力雄神社]] [[Category:2015年竣工の日本の建築物]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:多気町の建築物]]

It is also accessible via [[Mie Kotsu]] bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - {{Shrine | name = Sana Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sana Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location = [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki]], [[Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], Nita 156 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|51.3|N|136|32|46.1|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Enshrined Deities = [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]]
[[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] | type = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha (Minor)]], Former Prefectural Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = [[Shimmei-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Nakamiya, Ōmori Shrine | festival = [[October 8]] | Rituals = }} '''Sana Shrine''' (佐那神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki District]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine has historical ties to the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] and is associated with [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]], who is also enshrined as a [[secondary deity]] at the Ise Grand Shrine. == name and Etymology == The name "Sana" originates from the place name "Sana-no-agata" mentioned in ancient texts like the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Kotaijingu Gishikicho]]''.''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 411. In the [[Edo period]], the shrine was popularly called "Ōmori Shrine" or "Nakamiya." The name "Nakamiya" is believed to relate to its status as one of three prominent shrines in the region, alongside Sumaru-me Shrine (Ichinomiya) and [[Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)|Kushida Shrine]] (Taisha). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the following two deities: * [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]] * [[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] Ame-no-Tajikarao is mentioned in the ''Kojiki'' as residing in "Sana-no-agata," while Akebono-Tatsu-O is described as the ancestor of the [[Sana clan]]. Additional deities from merged shrines include: * [[Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[Kumano Gongen|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]] * [[Izanami-no-Mikoto]] * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] * [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] * [[Ame-no-Hohi]] * [[Amatsuhikone]] * [[Ikutsuhikone]] * [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano-Kusubi]] * [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Tagorihime]] * [[Ichikishima-hime]] * [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] * [[Ukanomitama]] * [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]] * [[Konohanasakuya-hime]] * [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Koto-shiro-nushi]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927) as a minor shrine (小社).''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 412. The shrine's main hall has historically been rebuilt using materials donated from the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] during its [[Jingū Shikinen Sengū|Shikinen Sengu]] reconstruction. In the Edo period, the shrine received official recognition as a ''Shikinaisha'' and was referred to as "Sana Shrine" on a stone marker erected in [[:en:1724|1724]]. It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in [[1943]]. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from [[Matsusaka]], [[Kameyama]], and as far as [[Nagoya]] and [[Osaka]].Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[JR Central|JR]] [[Kisei Main Line]] [[Sana Station]], approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. It is also accessible via [[Mie Kotsu]] bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sana Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Taki District|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki District|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|qq=}} ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagasaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakaniikawa District|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefecture|qq=}}) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Togami Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomei Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakyūshū City|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}) disambiguation]] [[Tajikarao Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|佐那神社|version=103551216|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sana Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tajikarao Shrine]] [[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:多気郡]] [[ja:三重県]] [[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] [[ja:尼崎市]] [[ja:兵庫県]] [[ja:雄山神社]] [[ja:立山町]] [[ja:上新川郡]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:北九州市]] [[ja:福岡県]] [[sn:Sana Shrine]] [[ja:手力雄神社]] [[Category:Built in Japan in 2015]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:多気町の建築物]] [[Category:多気町の歴史]]

{{Shrine | name = Sana Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sana Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location = [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki]], [[Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], Nita 156 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|51.3|N|136|32|46.1|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Enshrined Deities = [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]]
[[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] | type = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha (Minor)]], Former Prefectural Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = [[Shimmei-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Nakamiya, Ōmori Shrine | festival = [[October 8]] | Rituals = }} '''Sana Shrine''' (佐那神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki District]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine has historical ties to the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] and is associated with [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]], who is also enshrined as a [[secondary deity]] at the Ise Grand Shrine. == name and Etymology == The name "Sana" originates from the place name "Sana-no-agata" mentioned in ancient texts like the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Kotaijingu Gishikicho]]''.''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 411. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was popularly called "Ōmori Shrine" or "Nakamiya." The name "Nakamiya" is believed to relate to its status as one of three prominent shrines in the region, alongside Sumaru-me Shrine (Ichinomiya) and [[Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)|Kushida Shrine]] (Taisha). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the following two deities: * [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]] * [[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] Ame-no-Tajikarao is mentioned in the ''Kojiki'' as residing in "Sana-no-agata," while Akebono-Tatsu-O is described as the ancestor of the [[Sana clan]]. Additional deities from merged shrines include: * [[Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[Kumano Gongen|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]] * [[Izanami-no-Mikoto]] * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] * [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] * [[Ame-no-Hohi]] * [[Amatsuhikone]] * [[Ikutsuhikone]] * [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano-Kusubi]] * [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Tagorihime]] * [[Ichikishima-hime]] * [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] * [[Ukanomitama]] * [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]] * [[Konohanasakuya-hime]] * [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Koto-shiro-nushi]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927) as a minor shrine (小社).''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 412. The shrine's main hall has historically been rebuilt using materials donated from the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] during its [[Jingū Shikinen Sengū|Shikinen Sengu]] reconstruction. In the Edo period, the shrine received official recognition as a ''Shikinaisha'' and was referred to as "Sana Shrine" on a stone marker erected in [[:en:1724|1724]]. It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in [[:en:1943|1943]]. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from [[Matsusaka]], [[Kameyama]], and as far as [[Nagoya]] and [[Osaka]].Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[JR Central|JR]] [[Kisei Main Line]] [[Sana Station]], approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. It is also accessible via [[Mie Kotsu]] bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sana Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Taki District|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki District|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|qq=}} ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagasaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakaniikawa District|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefecture|qq=}}) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Togami Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomei Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakyūshū City|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}) disambiguation]] [[Tajikarao Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|佐那神社|version=103551216|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sana Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tajikarao Shrine]] [[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:多気郡]] [[ja:三重県]] [[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] [[ja:尼崎市]] [[ja:兵庫県]] [[ja:雄山神社]] [[ja:立山町]] [[ja:上新川郡]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:北九州市]] [[ja:福岡県]] [[sn:Sana Shrine]] [[ja:手力雄神社]] [[Category:Built in Japan in 2015]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:多気町の建築物]] [[Category:多気町の歴史]]

It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in [[:en:1943|1943]]. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from [[Matsusaka]], [[Kameyama]], and as far as [[Nagoya]] and [[Osaka]].Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[JR Central|JR]] [[Kisei Main Line]] [[Sana Station]], approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. It is also accessible via [[Mie Kotsu]] bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - {{Shrine | name = Sana Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sana Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki]], [[Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], Nita 156 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|51.3|N|136|32|46.1|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Enshrined Deities = [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]]
[[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] | type = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha (Minor)]], Former Prefectural Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = [[Shimmei-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Nakamiya, Ōmori Shrine | festival = [[October 8]] | Rituals = }} '''Sana Shrine''' (佐那神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki District]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine has historical ties to the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] and is associated with [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]], who is also enshrined as a [[secondary deity]] at the Ise Grand Shrine. == name and Etymology == The name "Sana" originates from the place name "Sana-no-agata" mentioned in ancient texts like the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Kotaijingu Gishikicho]]''.''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 411. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was popularly called "Ōmori Shrine" or "Nakamiya." The name "Nakamiya" is believed to relate to its status as one of three prominent shrines in the region, alongside Sumaru-me Shrine (Ichinomiya) and [[Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)|Kushida Shrine]] (Taisha). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the following two deities: * [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]] * [[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] Ame-no-Tajikarao is mentioned in the ''Kojiki'' as residing in "Sana-no-agata," while Akebono-Tatsu-O is described as the ancestor of the [[Sana clan]]. Additional deities from merged shrines include: * [[Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[Kumano Gongen|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]] * [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] * [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] * [[Ame-no-Hohi]] * [[Amatsuhikone]] * [[:en:Ikutsuhikone|Ikutsuhikone]] * [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano-Kusubi]] * [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Tagorihime]] * [[Ichikishima-hime]] * [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] * [[Ukanomitama]] * [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]] * [[Konohanasakuya-hime]] * [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Koto-shiro-nushi]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927) as a minor shrine (小社).''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 412. The shrine's main hall has historically been rebuilt using materials donated from the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] during its [[Jingū Shikinen Sengū|Shikinen Sengu]] reconstruction. In the Edo period, the shrine received official recognition as a ''Shikinaisha'' and was referred to as "Sana Shrine" on a stone marker erected in [[:en:1724|1724]]. It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in [[:en:1943|1943]]. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from [[Matsusaka]], [[Kameyama]], and as far as [[Nagoya]] and [[Osaka]].Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[JR Central|JR]] [[Kisei Main Line]] [[Sana Station]], approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. It is also accessible via [[Mie Kotsu]] bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sana Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Taki District|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki District|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|qq=}} ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagasaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakaniikawa District|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefecture|qq=}}) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Togami Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomei Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakyūshū City|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}) disambiguation]] [[Tajikarao Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|佐那神社|version=103551216|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sana Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tajikarao Shrine]] [[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:多気郡]] [[ja:三重県]] [[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] [[ja:尼崎市]] [[ja:兵庫県]] [[ja:雄山神社]] [[ja:立山町]] [[ja:上新川郡]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:北九州市]] [[ja:福岡県]] [[sn:Sana Shrine]] [[ja:手力雄神社]] [[Category:Built in Japan in 2015]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:多気町の建築物]] [[Category:多気町の歴史]]

Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sana Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Taki District|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki District|qq=}}, [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|qq=}} ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagasaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakaniikawa District|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefecture|qq=}}) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Togami Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomei Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakyūshū City|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}) disambiguation]] [[Tajikarao Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|佐那神社|version=103551216|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sana Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tajikarao Shrine]] [[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:多気郡]] [[ja:三重県]] [[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] [[ja:尼崎市]] [[ja:兵庫県]] [[ja:雄山神社]] [[ja:立山町]] [[ja:上新川郡]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:北九州市]] [[ja:福岡県]] [[sn:Sana Shrine]] [[ja:手力雄神社]] [[Category:Built in Japan in 2015]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:多気町の建築物]] [[Category:多気の Municipal History]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Sana Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sana Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki]], [[Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], Nita 156 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|51.3|N|136|32|46.1|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Enshrined Deities = [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]]
[[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] | type = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha (Minor)]], Former Prefectural Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = [[Shimmei-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Nakamiya, Ōmori Shrine | festival = [[October 8]] | Rituals = }} '''Sana Shrine''' (佐那神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki District]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine has historical ties to the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] and is associated with [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]], who is also enshrined as a [[secondary deity]] at the Ise Grand Shrine. == name and Etymology == The name "Sana" originates from the place name "Sana-no-agata" mentioned in ancient texts like the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Kotaijingu Gishikicho]]''.''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 411. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was popularly called "Ōmori Shrine" or "Nakamiya." The name "Nakamiya" is believed to relate to its status as one of three prominent shrines in the region, alongside Sumaru-me Shrine (Ichinomiya) and [[Kushida Shrine (Matsusaka)|Kushida Shrine]] (Taisha). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the following two deities: * [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]] * [[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] Ame-no-Tajikarao is mentioned in the ''Kojiki'' as residing in "Sana-no-agata," while Akebono-Tatsu-O is described as the ancestor of the [[Sana clan]]. Additional deities from merged shrines include: * [[Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[Kumano Gongen|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]] * [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] * [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] * [[Ame-no-Hohi]] * [[Amatsuhikone]] * [[:en:Ikutsuhikone|Ikutsuhikone]] * [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano-Kusubi]] * [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Tagorihime]] * [[Ichikishima-hime]] * [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] * [[Ukanomitama]] * [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]] * [[Konohanasakuya-hime]] * [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Koto-shiro-nushi]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927) as a minor shrine (小社).''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 412. The shrine's main hall has historically been rebuilt using materials donated from the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] during its [[Jingū Shikinen Sengū|Shikinen Sengu]] reconstruction. In the Edo period, the shrine received official recognition as a ''Shikinaisha'' and was referred to as "Sana Shrine" on a stone marker erected in [[:en:1724|1724]]. It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in [[:en:1943|1943]]. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from [[Matsusaka]], [[:en:Kameyama|Kameyama]], and as far as [[Nagoya]] and [[Osaka]].Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[JR Central|JR]] [[Kisei Main Line]] [[Sana Station]], approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. It is also accessible via [[Mie Kotsu]] bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sana Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Taki District|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki District|qq=}}, [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|qq=}} ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagasaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakaniikawa District|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefecture|qq=}}) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Togami Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomei Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakyūshū City|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}) disambiguation]] [[Tajikarao Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|佐那神社|version=103551216|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sana Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tajikarao Shrine]] [[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:多気郡]] [[ja:三重県]] [[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] [[ja:尼崎市]] [[ja:兵庫県]] [[ja:雄山神社]] [[ja:立山町]] [[ja:上新川郡]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:北九州市]] [[ja:福岡県]] [[sn:Sana Shrine]] [[ja:手力雄神社]] [[Category:Built in Japan in 2015]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:多気町の建築物]] [[Category:多気の Municipal History]]

{{Shrine | name = Sana Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sana Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki]], [[Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], Nita 156 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|51.3|N|136|32|46.1|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Enshrined Deities = [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]]
[[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] | type = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha (Minor)]], Former Prefectural Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = [[Shimmei-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Nakamiya, Ōmori Shrine | festival = [[October 8]] | Rituals = }} '''Sana Shrine''' (佐那神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki District]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine has historical ties to the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] and is associated with [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]], who is also enshrined as a [[secondary deity]] at the Ise Grand Shrine. == name and Etymology == The name "Sana" originates from the place name "Sana-no-agata" mentioned in ancient texts like the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Kotaijingu Gishikicho]]''.''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 411. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was popularly called "Ōmori Shrine" or "Nakamiya." The name "Nakamiya" is believed to relate to its status as one of three prominent shrines in the region, alongside Sumaru-me Shrine (Ichinomiya) and [[:en:Kushida |Kushida Shrine]] (Taisha). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the following two deities: * [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]] * [[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] Ame-no-Tajikarao is mentioned in the ''Kojiki'' as residing in "Sana-no-agata," while Akebono-Tatsu-O is described as the ancestor of the [[Sana clan]]. Additional deities from merged shrines include: * [[Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[Kumano Gongen|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]] * [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] * [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] * [[Ame-no-Hohi]] * [[Amatsuhikone]] * [[:en:Ikutsuhikone|Ikutsuhikone]] * [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano-Kusubi]] * [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Tagorihime]] * [[Ichikishima-hime]] * [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] * [[Ukanomitama]] * [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]] * [[Konohanasakuya-hime]] * [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Koto-shiro-nushi]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927) as a minor shrine (小社).''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 412. The shrine's main hall has historically been rebuilt using materials donated from the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] during its [[Jingū Shikinen Sengū|Shikinen Sengu]] reconstruction. In the Edo period, the shrine received official recognition as a ''Shikinaisha'' and was referred to as "Sana Shrine" on a stone marker erected in [[:en:1724|1724]]. It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in [[:en:1943|1943]]. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from [[Matsusaka]], [[:en:Kameyama|Kameyama]], and as far as [[Nagoya]] and [[Osaka]].Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[:en:JR Central|JR]] [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]] [[Sana Station]], approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. It is also accessible via [[Mie Kotsu]] bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sana Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Taki District|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki District|qq=}}, [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|qq=}} ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagasaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakaniikawa District|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefecture|qq=}}) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Togami Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomei Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakyūshū City|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}) disambiguation]] [[Tajikarao Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|佐那神社|version=103551216|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sana Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tajikarao Shrine]] [[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:多気郡]] [[ja:三重県]] [[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] [[ja:尼崎市]] [[ja:兵庫県]] [[ja:雄山神社]] [[ja:立山町]] [[ja:上新川郡]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:北九州市]] [[ja:福岡県]] [[sn:Sana Shrine]] [[ja:手力雄神社]] [[Category:Built in Japan in 2015]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:多気町の建築物]] [[Category:多気の Municipal History]]

{{Shrine | name = Sana Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sana Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki]], [[Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], Nita 156 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|51.3|N|136|32|46.1|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Enshrined Deities = [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]]
[[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] | type = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha (Minor)]], Former Prefectural Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = [[Shimmei-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Nakamiya, Ōmori Shrine | festival = [[October 8]] | Rituals = }} '''Sana Shrine''' (佐那神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki District]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine has historical ties to the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] and is associated with [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]], who is also enshrined as a [[secondary deity]] at the Ise Grand Shrine. == name and Etymology == The name "Sana" originates from the place name "Sana-no-agata" mentioned in ancient texts like the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Kotaijingu Gishikicho]]''.''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 411. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was popularly called "Ōmori Shrine" or "Nakamiya." The name "Nakamiya" is believed to relate to its status as one of three prominent shrines in the region, alongside Sumaru-me Shrine (Ichinomiya) and [[:en:Kushida |Kushida Shrine]] (Taisha). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the following two deities: * [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]] * [[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] Ame-no-Tajikarao is mentioned in the ''Kojiki'' as residing in "Sana-no-agata," while Akebono-Tatsu-O is described as the ancestor of the [[Sana clan]]. Additional deities from merged shrines include: * [[Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[Kumano Gongen|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]] * [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] * [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] * [[Ame-no-Hohi]] * [[Amatsuhikone]] * [[:en:Ikutsuhikone|Ikutsuhikone]] * [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano-Kusubi]] * [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Tagorihime]] * [[Ichikishima-hime]] * [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] * [[Ukanomitama]] * [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]] * [[Konohanasakuya-hime]] * [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Koto-shiro-nushi]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927) as a minor shrine (小社).''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 412. The shrine's main hall has historically been rebuilt using materials donated from the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] during its [[Jingū Shikinen Sengū|Shikinen Sengu]] reconstruction. In the Edo period, the shrine received official recognition as a ''Shikinaisha'' and was referred to as "Sana Shrine" on a stone marker erected in [[:en:1724|1724]]. It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in [[:en:1943|1943]]. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from [[Matsusaka]], [[:en:Kameyama|Kameyama]], and as far as [[Nagoya]] and [[Osaka]].Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[:en:JR Central|JR]] [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]] [[Sana Station]], approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. It is also accessible via [[Mie Kotsu]] bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sana Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Taki District|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki District|qq=}}, [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|qq=}} ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagasaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakaniikawa District|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefecture|qq=}}) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Togami Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomei Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakyūshū City|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}) disambiguation]] [[Tajikarao Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|佐那神社|version=103551216|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sana Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tajikarao Shrine]] [[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:多気郡]] [[ja:三重県]] [[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] [[ja:尼崎市]] [[ja:兵庫県]] [[ja:雄山神社]] [[ja:立山町]] [[ja:上新川郡]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:北九州市]] [[ja:福岡県]] [[sn:Sana Shrine]] [[ja:手力雄神社]] [[Category:Built in Japan in 2015]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:多気町の建築物]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taki, Mie Prefecture]]

It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in [[:en:1943|1943]]. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from [[:en:Matsusaka|Matsusaka]], [[:en:Kameyama|Kameyama]], and as far as [[Nagoya]] and [[Osaka]].Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[:en:JR Central|JR]] [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]] [[Sana Station]], approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. It is also accessible via [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - {{Shrine | name = Sana Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sana Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki]], [[Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], Nita 156 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|51.3|N|136|32|46.1|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Enshrined Deities = [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]]
[[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] | type = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha (Minor)]], Former Prefectural Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = [[Shimmei-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Nakamiya, Ōmori Shrine | festival = [[October 8]] | Rituals = }} '''Sana Shrine''' (佐那神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki District]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine has historical ties to the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] and is associated with [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]], who is also enshrined as a [[secondary deity]] at the Ise Grand Shrine. == name and Etymology == The name "Sana" originates from the place name "Sana-no-agata" mentioned in ancient texts like the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Kotaijingu Gishikicho]]''.''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 411. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was popularly called "Ōmori Shrine" or "Nakamiya." The name "Nakamiya" is believed to relate to its status as one of three prominent shrines in the region, alongside Sumaru-me Shrine (Ichinomiya) and [[:en:Kushida |Kushida Shrine]] (Taisha). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the following two deities: * [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]] * [[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] Ame-no-Tajikarao is mentioned in the ''Kojiki'' as residing in "Sana-no-agata," while Akebono-Tatsu-O is described as the ancestor of the [[Sana clan]]. Additional deities from merged shrines include: * [[Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[Kumano Gongen|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]] * [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] * [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] * [[Ame-no-Hohi]] * [[Amatsuhikone]] * [[:en:Ikutsuhikone|Ikutsuhikone]] * [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano-Kusubi]] * [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Tagorihime]] * [[Ichikishima-hime]] * [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] * [[Ukanomitama]] * [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]] * [[Konohanasakuya-hime]] * [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Koto-shiro-nushi]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927) as a minor shrine (小社).''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 412. The shrine's main hall has historically been rebuilt using materials donated from the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] during its [[Jingū Shikinen Sengū|Shikinen Sengu]] reconstruction. In the Edo period, the shrine received official recognition as a ''Shikinaisha'' and was referred to as "Sana Shrine" on a stone marker erected in [[:en:1724|1724]]. It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in [[:en:1943|1943]]. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from [[:en:Matsusaka|Matsusaka]], [[:en:Kameyama|Kameyama]], and as far as [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]] and [[Osaka]].Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[:en:JR Central|JR]] [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]] [[Sana Station]], approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. It is also accessible via [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sana Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Taki District|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki District|qq=}}, [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|qq=}} ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagasaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakaniikawa District|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefecture|qq=}}) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Togami Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomei Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakyūshū City|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}) disambiguation]] [[Tajikarao Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|佐那神社|version=103551216|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sana Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tajikarao Shrine]] [[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:多気郡]] [[ja:三重県]] [[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] [[ja:尼崎市]] [[ja:兵庫県]] [[ja:雄山神社]] [[ja:立山町]] [[ja:上新川郡]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:北九州市]] [[ja:福岡県]] [[sn:Sana Shrine]] [[ja:手力雄神社]] [[Category:Built in Japan in 2015]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:多気町の建築物]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taki, Mie Prefecture]]

Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sana Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Taki District|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki District|qq=}}, [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|qq=}} ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagasaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakaniikawa District|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefecture|qq=}}) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Togami Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomei Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakyūshū City|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}) disambiguation]] [[Tajikarao Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|佐那神社|version=103551216|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sana Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tajikarao Shrine]] [[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:多気郡]] [[ja:三重県]] [[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] [[ja:尼崎市]] [[ja:兵庫県]] [[ja:雄山神社]] [[ja:立山町]] [[ja:上新川郡]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:北九州市]] [[ja:福岡県]] [[sn:Sana Shrine]] [[ja:手力雄神社]] [[Category:Built in Japan in 2015]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:多気町の建築物]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taki, Mie Prefecture]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Sana Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sana Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki]], [[:en:Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], Nita 156 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|51.3|N|136|32|46.1|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Enshrined Deities = [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]]
[[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] | type = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha (Minor)]], Former Prefectural Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = [[Shimmei-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Nakamiya, Ōmori Shrine | festival = [[:en:October 8|October 8]] | Rituals = }} '''Sana Shrine''' (佐那神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], [[Taki District, Mie|Taki District]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine has historical ties to the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] and is associated with [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]], who is also enshrined as a [[secondary deity]] at the Ise Grand Shrine. == name and Etymology == The name "Sana" originates from the place name "Sana-no-agata" mentioned in ancient texts like the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Kotaijingu Gishikicho]]''.''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 411. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was popularly called "Ōmori Shrine" or "Nakamiya." The name "Nakamiya" is believed to relate to its status as one of three prominent shrines in the region, alongside Sumaru-me Shrine (Ichinomiya) and [[:en:Kushida |Kushida Shrine]] (Taisha). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the following two deities: * [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]] * [[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] Ame-no-Tajikarao is mentioned in the ''Kojiki'' as residing in "Sana-no-agata," while Akebono-Tatsu-O is described as the ancestor of the [[Sana clan]]. Additional deities from merged shrines include: * [[Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[Kumano Gongen|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]] * [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] * [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] * [[Ame-no-Hohi]] * [[Amatsuhikone]] * [[:en:Ikutsuhikone|Ikutsuhikone]] * [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano-Kusubi]] * [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Tagorihime]] * [[Ichikishima-hime]] * [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] * [[Ukanomitama]] * [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]] * [[Konohanasakuya-hime]] * [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Koto-shiro-nushi]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927) as a minor shrine (小社).''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 412. The shrine's main hall has historically been rebuilt using materials donated from the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] during its [[Jingū Shikinen Sengū|Shikinen Sengu]] reconstruction. In the Edo period, the shrine received official recognition as a ''Shikinaisha'' and was referred to as "Sana Shrine" on a stone marker erected in [[:en:1724|1724]]. It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in [[:en:1943|1943]]. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from [[:en:Matsusaka|Matsusaka]], [[:en:Kameyama|Kameyama]], and as far as [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]] and [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]].Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[:en:JR Central|JR]] [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]] [[Sana Station]], approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. It is also accessible via [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sana Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Taki District|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki District|qq=}}, [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|qq=}} ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagasaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakaniikawa District|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefecture|qq=}}) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Togami Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomei Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakyūshū City|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}) disambiguation]] [[Tajikarao Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|佐那神社|version=103551216|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sana Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tajikarao Shrine]] [[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:多気郡]] [[ja:三重県]] [[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] [[ja:尼崎市]] [[ja:兵庫県]] [[ja:雄山神社]] [[ja:立山町]] [[ja:上新川郡]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:北九州市]] [[ja:福岡県]] [[sn:Sana Shrine]] [[ja:手力雄神社]] [[Category:Built in Japan in 2015]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:多気町の建築物]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taki, Mie Prefecture]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Sana Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sana Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[:en:Taki District, Mie|Taki]], [[:en:Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], Nita 156 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|51.3|N|136|32|46.1|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Enshrined Deities = [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]]
[[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] | type = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha (Minor)]], Former Prefectural Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = [[Shimmei-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Nakamiya, Ōmori Shrine | festival = [[:en:October 8|October 8]] | Rituals = }} '''Sana Shrine''' (佐那神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Taki, Mie|Taki Town]], [[:en:Taki District, Mie|Taki District]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine has historical ties to the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] and is associated with [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]], who is also enshrined as a [[secondary deity]] at the Ise Grand Shrine. == name and Etymology == The name "Sana" originates from the place name "Sana-no-agata" mentioned in ancient texts like the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Kotaijingu Gishikicho]]''.''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 411. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was popularly called "Ōmori Shrine" or "Nakamiya." The name "Nakamiya" is believed to relate to its status as one of three prominent shrines in the region, alongside Sumaru-me Shrine (Ichinomiya) and [[:en:Kushida |Kushida Shrine]] (Taisha). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the following two deities: * [[:en:Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto]] * [[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] Ame-no-Tajikarao is mentioned in the ''Kojiki'' as residing in "Sana-no-agata," while Akebono-Tatsu-O is described as the ancestor of the [[Sana clan]]. Additional deities from merged shrines include: * [[Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[Kumano Gongen|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]] * [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] * [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] * [[Ame-no-Hohi]] * [[Amatsuhikone]] * [[:en:Ikutsuhikone|Ikutsuhikone]] * [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano-Kusubi]] * [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Tagorihime]] * [[Ichikishima-hime]] * [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] * [[Ukanomitama]] * [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]] * [[Konohanasakuya-hime]] * [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Koto-shiro-nushi]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] * [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927) as a minor shrine (小社).''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 412. The shrine's main hall has historically been rebuilt using materials donated from the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] during its [[Jingū Shikinen Sengū|Shikinen Sengu]] reconstruction. In the Edo period, the shrine received official recognition as a ''Shikinaisha'' and was referred to as "Sana Shrine" on a stone marker erected in [[:en:1724|1724]]. It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in [[:en:1943|1943]]. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from [[:en:Matsusaka|Matsusaka]], [[:en:Kameyama|Kameyama]], and as far as [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]] and [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]].Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[:en:JR Central|JR]] [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]] [[:en:Sana Station|Sana Station]], approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. It is also accessible via [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sana Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Taki District|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki District|qq=}}, [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|qq=}} ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagasaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakaniikawa District|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefecture|qq=}}) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Togami Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomei Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakyūshū City|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}) disambiguation]] [[Tajikarao Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|佐那神社|version=103551216|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sana Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tajikarao Shrine]] [[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:多気郡]] [[ja:三重県]] [[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] [[ja:尼崎市]] [[ja:兵庫県]] [[ja:雄山神社]] [[ja:立山町]] [[ja:上新川郡]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:北九州市]] [[ja:福岡県]] [[sn:Sana Shrine]] [[ja:手力雄神社]] [[Category:Built in Japan in 2015]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:多気町の建築物]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taki, Mie Prefecture]]
