[[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:佐那神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17226795]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sana Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Sana Shrine.jpg | location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Taki District, Mie|en|Taki District, Mie|WD=Q1042360|lt=Taki}}, {{ill|Taki, Mie|en|Taki, Mie|WD=Q1203963|lt=Taki Town}}, Nita 156 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.480917|N|136.546139|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto}}
[[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] | type = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha (Minor)]], Former Prefectural Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = [[Shimmei-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Nakamiya, Ōmori Shrine | festival = {{ill|October 8|en|October 8|WD=Q2924|lt=October 8}} | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Taki|WD=Q1203963}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-tajikarao|WD=Q849412}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Sana Shrine''' (佐那神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Taki, Mie|en|Taki, Mie|WD=Q1203963|lt=Taki Town}}, {{ill|Taki District, Mie|en|Taki District, Mie|WD=Q1042360|lt=Taki District}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan. The shrine has historical ties to the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] and is associated with {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto}}, who is also enshrined as a [[secondary deity]] at the Ise Grand Shrine. == name and Etymology == The name "Sana" originates from the place name "Sana-no-agata" mentioned in ancient texts like the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Kotaijingu Gishikicho]]''.''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 411. In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the shrine was popularly called "Ōmori Shrine" or "Nakamiya." The name "Nakamiya" is believed to relate to its status as one of three prominent shrines in the region, alongside Sumaru-me Shrine (Ichinomiya) and {{ill|Kushida |en|Kushida |WD=Q1794549|lt=Kushida Shrine}} (Taisha). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the following two deities: * {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto}} * [[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] Ame-no-Tajikarao is mentioned in the ''Kojiki'' as residing in "Sana-no-agata," while Akebono-Tatsu-O is described as the ancestor of the [[Sana clan]]. Additional deities from merged shrines include: * [[Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[Kumano Gongen|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]] * {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami}} * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] * [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] * [[Ame-no-Hohi]] * [[Amatsuhikone]] * {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|en|Ikutsuhikone|WD=Q11147583|lt=Ikutsuhikone}} * [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano-Kusubi]] * [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Tagorihime]] * [[Ichikishima-hime]] * [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] * [[Ukanomitama]] * [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]] * [[Konohanasakuya-hime]] * [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Koto-shiro-nushi]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}} == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927) as a minor shrine (小社).''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 412. The shrine's main hall has historically been rebuilt using materials donated from the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] during its [[Jingū Shikinen Sengū|Shikinen Sengu]] reconstruction. In the Edo period, the shrine received official recognition as a ''Shikinaisha'' and was referred to as "Sana Shrine" on a stone marker erected in {{ill|1724|en|1724|WD=Q6926|lt=1724}}. It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in {{ill|1943|en|1943|WD=Q18623|lt=1943}}. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from {{ill|Matsusaka|en|Matsusaka|WD=Q460829|lt=Matsusaka}}, {{ill|Kameyama|en|Kameyama|WD=Q448668|lt=Kameyama}}, and as far as {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya}} and {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka}}.Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[:en:JR Central|JR]] {{ill|Kisei Main Line|en|Kisei Main Line|WD=Q582803|lt=Kisei Main Line}} {{ill|Sana Station|en|Sana Station|WD=Q265438|lt=Sana Station}}, approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. It is also accessible via {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu}} bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sana Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Taki District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki District|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|qq=}} ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagasaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakaniikawa District|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefecture|qq=}}) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Togami Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomei Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakyūshū City|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}) disambiguation]] [[Tajikarao Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|佐那神社|version=103551216|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:多気郡]] [[ja:三重県]] [[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] [[ja:尼崎市]] [[ja:兵庫県]] [[ja:雄山神社]] [[ja:立山町]] [[ja:上新川郡]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:北九州市]] [[ja:福岡県]] [[ja:手力雄神社]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Sana Shrine (Q17226795)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! It is also accessible via {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu}} bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sana Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Taki District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki District|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|qq=}} ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagasaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakaniikawa District|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefecture|qq=}}) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Togami Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomei Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakyūshū City|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}) disambiguation]] [[Tajikarao Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|佐那神社|version=103551216|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:多気郡]] [[ja:三重県]] [[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] [[ja:尼崎市]] [[ja:兵庫県]] [[ja:雄山神社]] [[ja:立山町]] [[ja:上新川郡]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:北九州市]] [[ja:福岡県]] [[ja:手力雄神社]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Sana Shrine (Q17226795)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. revision.text:
[[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:佐那神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17226795]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sana Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Sana Shrine.jpg | location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Taki District, Mie|en|Taki District, Mie|WD=Q1042360|lt=Taki}}, {{ill|Taki, Mie|en|Taki, Mie|WD=Q1203963|lt=Taki Town}}, Nita 156 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.480917|N|136.546139|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto}}
[[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] | type = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha (Minor)]], Former Prefectural Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = [[Shimmei-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Nakamiya, Ōmori Shrine | festival = {{ill|October 8|en|October 8|WD=Q2924|lt=October 8}} | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Taki|WD=Q1203963}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-tajikarao|WD=Q849412}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Sana Shrine''' (佐那神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Taki, Mie|en|Taki, Mie|WD=Q1203963|lt=Taki Town}}, {{ill|Taki District, Mie|en|Taki District, Mie|WD=Q1042360|lt=Taki District}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan. The shrine has historical ties to the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] and is associated with {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto}}, who is also enshrined as a [[secondary deity]] at the Ise Grand Shrine. == name and Etymology == The name "Sana" originates from the place name "Sana-no-agata" mentioned in ancient texts like the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Kotaijingu Gishikicho]]''.''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 411. In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the shrine was popularly called "Ōmori Shrine" or "Nakamiya." The name "Nakamiya" is believed to relate to its status as one of three prominent shrines in the region, alongside Sumaru-me Shrine (Ichinomiya) and {{ill|Kushida |en|Kushida |WD=Q1794549|lt=Kushida Shrine}} (Taisha). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the following two deities: * {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto}} * [[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] Ame-no-Tajikarao is mentioned in the ''Kojiki'' as residing in "Sana-no-agata," while Akebono-Tatsu-O is described as the ancestor of the [[Sana clan]]. Additional deities from merged shrines include: * [[Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[Kumano Gongen|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]] * {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami}} * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] * [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] * [[Ame-no-Hohi]] * [[Amatsuhikone]] * {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|en|Ikutsuhikone|WD=Q11147583|lt=Ikutsuhikone}} * [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano-Kusubi]] * [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Tagorihime]] * [[Ichikishima-hime]] * [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] * [[Ukanomitama]] * [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]] * [[Konohanasakuya-hime]] * [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Koto-shiro-nushi]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}} == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927) as a minor shrine (小社).''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 412. The shrine's main hall has historically been rebuilt using materials donated from the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] during its [[Jingū Shikinen Sengū|Shikinen Sengu]] reconstruction. In the Edo period, the shrine received official recognition as a ''Shikinaisha'' and was referred to as "Sana Shrine" on a stone marker erected in {{ill|1724|en|1724|WD=Q6926|lt=1724}}. It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in {{ill|1943|en|1943|WD=Q18623|lt=1943}}. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from {{ill|Matsusaka|en|Matsusaka|WD=Q460829|lt=Matsusaka}}, {{ill|Kameyama|en|Kameyama|WD=Q448668|lt=Kameyama}}, and as far as {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya}} and {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka}}.Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[:en:JR Central|JR]] {{ill|Kisei Main Line|en|Kisei Main Line|WD=Q582803|lt=Kisei Main Line}} {{ill|Sana Station|en|Sana Station|WD=Q265438|lt=Sana Station}}, approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. It is also accessible via {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu}} bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sana Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Taki District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki District|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|qq=}} ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagasaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakaniikawa District|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefecture|qq=}}) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Togami Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomei Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakyūshū City|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}) disambiguation]] [[Tajikarao Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|佐那神社|version=103551216|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:多気郡]] [[ja:三重県]] [[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] [[ja:尼崎市]] [[ja:兵庫県]] [[ja:雄山神社]] [[ja:立山町]] [[ja:上新川郡]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:北九州市]] [[ja:福岡県]] [[ja:手力雄神社]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Sana Shrine (Q17226795)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Taki District, Mie|en|Taki District, Mie|ja|多気郡|ceb|Taki-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی تاکی|es|Distrito de Taki|fr|District de Taki|id|Distrik Taki, Mie|it|Distretto di Taki|ko|다키군|pl|Powiat Taki (Mie)|uk|Повіт Такі|zh|多氣郡|lt=Taki district|WD=Q1042360}} || {{ill|Sana Shrine|lt=Sana Shrine|ja|佐那神社|WD=Q17226795}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287}} || 3 seats || — || {{coord|34.480942|136.546151|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181160 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17226795}}==Notes== [[Category:Buildings in Japan completed in 2015]] [[Category:Buildings in Taki Town]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taki, Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:pages with former wikipedia userpage links]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

revision.text:
[[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:佐那神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sana Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Sana Shrine.jpg | location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Taki District, Mie|en|Taki District, Mie|WD=Q1042360|lt=Taki}}, {{ill|Taki, Mie|en|Taki, Mie|WD=Q1203963|lt=Taki Town}}, Nita 156 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.480917|N|136.546139|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto}}
[[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] | type = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha (Minor)]], Former Prefectural Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = [[Shimmei-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Nakamiya, Ōmori Shrine | festival = {{ill|October 8|en|October 8|WD=Q2924|lt=October 8}} | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Taki|WD=Q1203963}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-tajikarao|WD=Q849412}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Sana Shrine''' (佐那神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Taki, Mie|en|Taki, Mie|WD=Q1203963|lt=Taki Town}}, {{ill|Taki District, Mie|en|Taki District, Mie|WD=Q1042360|lt=Taki District}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan. The shrine has historical ties to the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] and is associated with {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto}}, who is also enshrined as a [[secondary deity]] at the Ise Grand Shrine. == name and Etymology == The name "Sana" originates from the place name "Sana-no-agata" mentioned in ancient texts like the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Kotaijingu Gishikicho]]''.''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 411. In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the shrine was popularly called "Ōmori Shrine" or "Nakamiya." The name "Nakamiya" is believed to relate to its status as one of three prominent shrines in the region, alongside Sumaru-me Shrine (Ichinomiya) and {{ill|Kushida |en|Kushida |WD=Q1794549|lt=Kushida Shrine}} (Taisha). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the following two deities: * {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto}} * [[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] Ame-no-Tajikarao is mentioned in the ''Kojiki'' as residing in "Sana-no-agata," while Akebono-Tatsu-O is described as the ancestor of the [[Sana clan]]. Additional deities from merged shrines include: * [[Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[Kumano Gongen|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]] * {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami}} * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] * [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] * [[Ame-no-Hohi]] * [[Amatsuhikone]] * {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|en|Ikutsuhikone|WD=Q11147583|lt=Ikutsuhikone}} * [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano-Kusubi]] * [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Tagorihime]] * [[Ichikishima-hime]] * [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] * [[Ukanomitama]] * [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]] * [[Konohanasakuya-hime]] * [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Koto-shiro-nushi]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}} == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927) as a minor shrine (小社).''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 412. The shrine's main hall has historically been rebuilt using materials donated from the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] during its [[Jingū Shikinen Sengū|Shikinen Sengu]] reconstruction. In the Edo period, the shrine received official recognition as a ''Shikinaisha'' and was referred to as "Sana Shrine" on a stone marker erected in {{ill|1724|en|1724|WD=Q6926|lt=1724}}. It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in {{ill|1943|en|1943|WD=Q18623|lt=1943}}. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from {{ill|Matsusaka|en|Matsusaka|WD=Q460829|lt=Matsusaka}}, {{ill|Kameyama|en|Kameyama|WD=Q448668|lt=Kameyama}}, and as far as {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya}} and {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka}}.Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[:en:JR Central|JR]] {{ill|Kisei Main Line|en|Kisei Main Line|WD=Q582803|lt=Kisei Main Line}} {{ill|Sana Station|en|Sana Station|WD=Q265438|lt=Sana Station}}, approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. It is also accessible via {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu}} bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sana Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Taki District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki District|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|qq=}} ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagasaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakaniikawa District|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefecture|qq=}}) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Togami Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomei Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakyūshū City|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}) disambiguation]] [[Tajikarao Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|佐那神社|version=103551216|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:多気郡]] [[ja:三重県]] [[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] [[ja:尼崎市]] [[ja:兵庫県]] [[ja:雄山神社]] [[ja:立山町]] [[ja:上新川郡]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:北九州市]] [[ja:福岡県]] [[ja:手力雄神社]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Sana Shrine (Q17226795)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Taki District, Mie|en|Taki District, Mie|ja|多気郡|ceb|Taki-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی تاکی|es|Distrito de Taki|fr|District de Taki|id|Distrik Taki, Mie|it|Distretto di Taki|ko|다키군|pl|Powiat Taki (Mie)|uk|Повіт Такі|zh|多氣郡|lt=Taki district|WD=Q1042360}} || {{ill|Sana Shrine|lt=Sana Shrine|ja|佐那神社|WD=Q17226795}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287}} || 3 seats || — || {{coord|34.480942|136.546151|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181160 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17226795}}==Notes== [[Category:Buildings in Japan completed in 2015]] [[Category:Buildings in Taki Town]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taki, Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:pages with former wikipedia userpage links]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

In the Edo period, the shrine received official recognition as a ''Shikinaisha'' and was referred to as "Sana Shrine" on a stone marker erected in {{ill|1724|en|1724|WD=Q6926|lt=1724}}. It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in {{ill|1943|en|1943|WD=Q18623|lt=1943}}. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from {{ill|Matsusaka|en|Matsusaka|WD=Q460829|lt=Matsusaka}}, {{ill|Kameyama|en|Kameyama|WD=Q448668|lt=Kameyama}}, and as far as {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya}} and {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka}}.Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[:en:JR Central|JR]] {{ill|Kisei Main Line|en|Kisei Main Line|WD=Q582803|lt=Kisei Main Line}} {{ill|Sana Station|en|Sana Station|WD=Q265438|lt=Sana Station}}, approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. It is also accessible via {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu}} bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sana Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Taki District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki District|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|qq=}} ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagasaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakaniikawa District|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefecture|qq=}}) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Togami Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomei Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakyūshū City|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}) disambiguation]] [[Tajikarao Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|佐那神社|version=103551216|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:多気郡]] [[ja:三重県]] [[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] [[ja:尼崎市]] [[ja:兵庫県]] [[ja:雄山神社]] [[ja:立山町]] [[ja:上新川郡]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:北九州市]] [[ja:福岡県]] [[ja:手力雄神社]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Sana Shrine (Q17226795)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. The shrine's main hall has historically been rebuilt using materials donated from the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] during its [[Jingū Shikinen Sengū|Shikinen Sengu]] reconstruction. It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in {{ill|1943|en|1943|WD=Q18623|lt=1943}}. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from {{ill|Matsusaka|en|Matsusaka|WD=Q460829|lt=Matsusaka}}, {{ill|Kameyama|en|Kameyama|WD=Q448668|lt=Kameyama}}, and as far as {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya}} and {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka}}.Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[:en:JR Central|JR]] {{ill|Kisei Main Line|en|Kisei Main Line|WD=Q582803|lt=Kisei Main Line}} {{ill|Sana Station|en|Sana Station|WD=Q265438|lt=Sana Station}}, approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. It is also accessible via {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu}} bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sana Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Taki District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki District|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|qq=}} ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagasaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakaniikawa District|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefecture|qq=}}) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Togami Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomei Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakyūshū City|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}) disambiguation]] [[Tajikarao Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|佐那神社|version=103551216|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:多気郡]] [[ja:三重県]] [[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] [[ja:尼崎市]] [[ja:兵庫県]] [[ja:雄山神社]] [[ja:立山町]] [[ja:上新川郡]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:北九州市]] [[ja:福岡県]] [[ja:手力雄神社]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Sana Shrine (Q17226795)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! [[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:佐那神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sana Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Sana Shrine.jpg | location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Taki District, Mie|en|Taki District, Mie|WD=Q1042360|lt=Taki|1=Taki District, Mie}}, {{ill|Taki, Mie|en|Taki, Mie|WD=Q1203963|lt=Taki Town|1=Taki, Mie}}, Nita 156 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.480917|N|136.546139|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}}
[[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] | type = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha (Minor)]], Former Prefectural Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = [[Shimmei-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Nakamiya, Ōmori Shrine | festival = {{ill|October 8|en|October 8|WD=Q2924|lt=October 8|1=October 8}} | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Taki|WD=Q1203963|1=Taki, Mie}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-tajikarao|WD=Q849412|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Sana Shrine''' (佐那神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Taki, Mie|en|Taki, Mie|WD=Q1203963|lt=Taki Town|1=Taki, Mie}}, {{ill|Taki District, Mie|en|Taki District, Mie|WD=Q1042360|lt=Taki District|1=Taki District, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan. The shrine has historical ties to the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] and is associated with {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}}, who is also enshrined as a [[secondary deity]] at the Ise Grand Shrine. == name and Etymology == The name "Sana" originates from the place name "Sana-no-agata" mentioned in ancient texts like the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Kotaijingu Gishikicho]]''.''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 411. In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was popularly called "Ōmori Shrine" or "Nakamiya." The name "Nakamiya" is believed to relate to its status as one of three prominent shrines in the region, alongside Sumaru-me Shrine (Ichinomiya) and {{ill|Kushida |en|Kushida |WD=Q1794549|lt=Kushida Shrine|1=Kushida}} (Taisha). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the following two deities: * {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} * [[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] Ame-no-Tajikarao is mentioned in the ''Kojiki'' as residing in "Sana-no-agata," while Akebono-Tatsu-O is described as the ancestor of the [[Sana clan]]. Additional deities from merged shrines include: * [[Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[Kumano Gongen|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]] * {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}} * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] * [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] * [[Ame-no-Hohi]] * [[Amatsuhikone]] * {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|en|Ikutsuhikone|WD=Q11147583|lt=Ikutsuhikone|1=Ikutsuhikone}} * [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano-Kusubi]] * [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Tagorihime]] * [[Ichikishima-hime]] * [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] * [[Ukanomitama]] * [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]] * [[Konohanasakuya-hime]] * [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Koto-shiro-nushi]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927) as a minor shrine (小社).''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 412. The shrine's main hall has historically been rebuilt using materials donated from the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] during its [[Jingū Shikinen Sengū|Shikinen Sengu]] reconstruction. In the Edo period, the shrine received official recognition as a ''Shikinaisha'' and was referred to as "Sana Shrine" on a stone marker erected in {{ill|1724|en|1724|WD=Q6926|lt=1724|1=1724}}. It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in {{ill|1943|en|1943|WD=Q18623|lt=1943|1=1943}}. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from {{ill|Matsusaka|en|Matsusaka|WD=Q460829|lt=Matsusaka|1=Matsusaka}}, {{ill|Kameyama|en|Kameyama|WD=Q448668|lt=Kameyama|1=Kameyama}}, and as far as {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya|1=Nagoya}} and {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka|1=Osaka}}.Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[:en:JR Central|JR]] {{ill|Kisei Main Line|en|Kisei Main Line|WD=Q582803|lt=Kisei Main Line|1=Kisei Main Line}} {{ill|Sana Station|en|Sana Station|WD=Q265438|lt=Sana Station|1=Sana Station}}, approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. It is also accessible via {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu|1=Mie Kotsu}} bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sana Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Taki District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki District|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|qq=}} ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagasaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakaniikawa District|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefecture|qq=}}) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Togami Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomei Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakyūshū City|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}) disambiguation]] [[Tajikarao Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|佐那神社|version=103551216|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:多気郡]] [[ja:三重県]] [[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] [[ja:尼崎市]] [[ja:兵庫県]] [[ja:雄山神社]] [[ja:立山町]] [[ja:上新川郡]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:北九州市]] [[ja:福岡県]] [[ja:手力雄神社]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Sana Shrine (Q17226795)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Taki District, Mie|en|Taki District, Mie|ja|多気郡|ceb|Taki-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی تاکی|es|Distrito de Taki|fr|District de Taki|id|Distrik Taki, Mie|it|Distretto di Taki|ko|다키군|pl|Powiat Taki (Mie)|uk|Повіт Такі|zh|多氣郡|lt=Taki district|WD=Q1042360|1=Taki District, Mie}} || {{ill|Sana Shrine|lt=Sana Shrine|ja|佐那神社|WD=Q17226795|1=Sana Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || 3 seats || — || {{coord|34.480942|136.546151|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181160 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17226795}}==Notes== [[Category:Buildings in Japan completed in 2015]] [[Category:Buildings in Taki Town]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha merges]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taki, Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:pages with former wikipedia userpage links]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in {{ill|1943|en|1943|WD=Q18623|lt=1943|1=1943}}. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from {{ill|Matsusaka|en|Matsusaka|WD=Q460829|lt=Matsusaka|1=Matsusaka}}, {{ill|Kameyama|en|Kameyama|WD=Q448668|lt=Kameyama|1=Kameyama}}, and as far as {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya|1=Nagoya}} and {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka|1=Osaka}}.Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[:en:JR Central|JR]] {{ill|Kisei Main Line|en|Kisei Main Line|WD=Q582803|lt=Kisei Main Line|1=Kisei Main Line}} {{ill|Sana Station|en|Sana Station|WD=Q265438|lt=Sana Station|1=Sana Station}}, approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. It is also accessible via {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu|1=Mie Kotsu}} bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sana Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Taki District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki District|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|qq=}} ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagasaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakaniikawa District|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefecture|qq=}}) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Togami Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomei Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakyūshū City|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}) disambiguation]] [[Tajikarao Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|佐那神社|version=103551216|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:多気郡]] [[ja:三重県]] [[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] [[ja:尼崎市]] [[ja:兵庫県]] [[ja:雄山神社]] [[ja:立山町]] [[ja:上新川郡]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:北九州市]] [[ja:福岡県]] [[ja:手力雄神社]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Sana Shrine (Q17226795)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Taki District, Mie|en|Taki District, Mie|ja|多気郡|ceb|Taki-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی تاکی|es|Distrito de Taki|fr|District de Taki|id|Distrik Taki, Mie|it|Distretto di Taki|ko|다키군|pl|Powiat Taki (Mie)|uk|Повіт Такі|zh|多氣郡|lt=Taki district|WD=Q1042360|1=Taki District, Mie}} || {{ill|Sana Shrine|lt=Sana Shrine|ja|佐那神社|WD=Q17226795|1=Sana Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || 3 seats || — || {{coord|34.480942|136.546151|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181160 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17226795}}==Notes== [[Category:Buildings in Japan completed in 2015]] [[Category:Buildings in Taki Town]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taki, Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:pages with former wikipedia userpage links]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:佐那神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sana Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Sana Shrine.jpg | location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Taki District, Mie|en|Taki District, Mie|WD=Q1042360|lt=Taki|1=Taki District, Mie}}, {{ill|Taki, Mie|en|Taki, Mie|WD=Q1203963|lt=Taki Town|1=Taki, Mie}}, Nita 156 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.480917|N|136.546139|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}}
[[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] | type = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha (Minor)]], Former Prefectural Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = [[Shimmei-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Nakamiya, Ōmori Shrine | festival = {{ill|October 8|en|October 8|WD=Q2924|lt=October 8|1=October 8}} | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Taki|WD=Q1203963|1=Taki, Mie}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-tajikarao|WD=Q849412|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Sana Shrine''' (佐那神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Taki, Mie|en|Taki, Mie|WD=Q1203963|lt=Taki Town|1=Taki, Mie}}, {{ill|Taki District, Mie|en|Taki District, Mie|WD=Q1042360|lt=Taki District|1=Taki District, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan. The shrine has historical ties to the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] and is associated with {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}}, who is also enshrined as a [[secondary deity]] at the Ise Grand Shrine. == name and Etymology == The name "Sana" originates from the place name "Sana-no-agata" mentioned in ancient texts like the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Kotaijingu Gishikicho]]''.''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 411. In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was popularly called "Ōmori Shrine" or "Nakamiya." The name "Nakamiya" is believed to relate to its status as one of three prominent shrines in the region, alongside Sumaru-me Shrine (Ichinomiya) and {{ill|Kushida |en|Kushida |WD=Q1794549|lt=Kushida Shrine|1=Kushida}} (Taisha). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the following two deities: * {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} * [[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] Ame-no-Tajikarao is mentioned in the ''Kojiki'' as residing in "Sana-no-agata," while Akebono-Tatsu-O is described as the ancestor of the [[Sana clan]]. Additional deities from merged shrines include: * [[Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[Kumano Gongen|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]] * {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}} * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] * [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] * [[Ame-no-Hohi]] * [[Amatsuhikone]] * {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|en|Ikutsuhikone|WD=Q11147583|lt=Ikutsuhikone|1=Ikutsuhikone}} * [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano-Kusubi]] * [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Tagorihime]] * [[Ichikishima-hime]] * [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] * [[Ukanomitama]] * [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]] * [[Konohanasakuya-hime]] * [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Koto-shiro-nushi]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927) as a minor shrine (小社).''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 412. The shrine's main hall has historically been rebuilt using materials donated from the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] during its [[Jingū Shikinen Sengū|Shikinen Sengu]] reconstruction. In the Edo period, the shrine received official recognition as a ''Shikinaisha'' and was referred to as "Sana Shrine" on a stone marker erected in {{ill|1724|en|1724|WD=Q6926|lt=1724|1=1724}}. It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in {{ill|1943|en|1943|WD=Q18623|lt=1943|1=1943}}. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from {{ill|Matsusaka|en|Matsusaka|WD=Q460829|lt=Matsusaka|1=Matsusaka}}, {{ill|Kameyama|en|Kameyama|WD=Q448668|lt=Kameyama|1=Kameyama}}, and as far as {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya|1=Nagoya}} and {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka|1=Osaka}}.Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[:en:JR Central|JR]] {{ill|Kisei Main Line|en|Kisei Main Line|WD=Q582803|lt=Kisei Main Line|1=Kisei Main Line}} {{ill|Sana Station|en|Sana Station|WD=Q265438|lt=Sana Station|1=Sana Station}}, approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. It is also accessible via {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu|1=Mie Kotsu}} bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sana Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Taki District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki District|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|qq=}} ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagasaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakaniikawa District|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefecture|qq=}}) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Togami Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomei Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakyūshū City|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}) disambiguation]] [[Tajikarao Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|佐那神社|version=103551216|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:多気郡]] [[ja:三重県]] [[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] [[ja:尼崎市]] [[ja:兵庫県]] [[ja:雄山神社]] [[ja:立山町]] [[ja:上新川郡]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:北九州市]] [[ja:福岡県]] [[ja:手力雄神社]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Sana Shrine (Q17226795)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Taki District, Mie|en|Taki District, Mie|ja|多気郡|ceb|Taki-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی تاکی|es|Distrito de Taki|fr|District de Taki|id|Distrik Taki, Mie|it|Distretto di Taki|ko|다키군|pl|Powiat Taki (Mie)|uk|Повіт Такі|zh|多氣郡|lt=Taki district|WD=Q1042360|1=Taki District, Mie}} || {{ill|Sana Shrine|lt=Sana Shrine|ja|佐那神社|WD=Q17226795|1=Sana Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || 3 seats || — || {{coord|34.480942|136.546151|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181160 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17226795}}==Notes== [[Category:Buildings in Japan completed in 2015]] [[Category:Buildings in Taki Town]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taki, Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:pages with former wikipedia userpage links]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in {{ill|1943|en|1943|WD=Q18623|lt=1943|1=1943}}. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from {{ill|Matsusaka|en|Matsusaka|WD=Q460829|lt=Matsusaka|1=Matsusaka}}, {{ill|Kameyama|en|Kameyama|WD=Q448668|lt=Kameyama|1=Kameyama}}, and as far as {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya|1=Nagoya}} and {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka|1=Osaka}}.Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[:en:JR Central|JR]] {{ill|Kisei Main Line|en|Kisei Main Line|WD=Q582803|lt=Kisei Main Line|1=Kisei Main Line}} {{ill|Sana Station|en|Sana Station|WD=Q265438|lt=Sana Station|1=Sana Station}}, approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. It is also accessible via {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu|1=Mie Kotsu}} bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sana Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Taki District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki District|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|qq=}} ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagasaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakaniikawa District|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefecture|qq=}}) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Togami Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomei Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakyūshū City|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}) disambiguation]] [[Tajikarao Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|佐那神社|version=103551216|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:多気郡]] [[ja:三重県]] [[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] [[ja:尼崎市]] [[ja:兵庫県]] [[ja:雄山神社]] [[ja:立山町]] [[ja:上新川郡]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:北九州市]] [[ja:福岡県]] [[ja:手力雄神社]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Sana Shrine (Q17226795)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. revision.text:
[[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:佐那神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sana Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Sana Shrine.jpg | location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Taki District, Mie|en|Taki District, Mie|WD=Q1042360|lt=Taki|1=Taki District, Mie}}, {{ill|Taki, Mie|en|Taki, Mie|WD=Q1203963|lt=Taki Town|1=Taki, Mie}}, Nita 156 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.480917|N|136.546139|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}}
[[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] | type = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha (Minor)]], Former Prefectural Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = [[Shimmei-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Nakamiya, Ōmori Shrine | festival = {{ill|October 8|en|October 8|WD=Q2924|lt=October 8|1=October 8}} | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Taki|WD=Q1203963|1=Taki, Mie}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-tajikarao|WD=Q849412|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Sana Shrine''' (佐那神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Taki, Mie|en|Taki, Mie|WD=Q1203963|lt=Taki Town|1=Taki, Mie}}, {{ill|Taki District, Mie|en|Taki District, Mie|WD=Q1042360|lt=Taki District|1=Taki District, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan. The shrine has historical ties to the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] and is associated with {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}}, who is also enshrined as a [[secondary deity]] at the Ise Grand Shrine. == name and Etymology == The name "Sana" originates from the place name "Sana-no-agata" mentioned in ancient texts like the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Kotaijingu Gishikicho]]''.''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 411. In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was popularly called "Ōmori Shrine" or "Nakamiya." The name "Nakamiya" is believed to relate to its status as one of three prominent shrines in the region, alongside Sumaru-me Shrine (Ichinomiya) and {{ill|Kushida |en|Kushida |WD=Q1794549|lt=Kushida Shrine|1=Kushida}} (Taisha). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the following two deities: * {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} * [[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] Ame-no-Tajikarao is mentioned in the ''Kojiki'' as residing in "Sana-no-agata," while Akebono-Tatsu-O is described as the ancestor of the [[Sana clan]]. Additional deities from merged shrines include: * [[Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[Kumano Gongen|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]] * {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}} * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] * [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] * [[Ame-no-Hohi]] * [[Amatsuhikone]] * {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|en|Ikutsuhikone|WD=Q11147583|lt=Ikutsuhikone|1=Ikutsuhikone}} * [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano-Kusubi]] * [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Tagorihime]] * [[Ichikishima-hime]] * [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] * [[Ukanomitama]] * [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]] * [[Konohanasakuya-hime]] * [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Koto-shiro-nushi]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927) as a minor shrine (小社).''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 412. The shrine's main hall has historically been rebuilt using materials donated from the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] during its [[Jingū Shikinen Sengū|Shikinen Sengu]] reconstruction. In the Edo period, the shrine received official recognition as a ''Shikinaisha'' and was referred to as "Sana Shrine" on a stone marker erected in {{ill|1724|en|1724|WD=Q6926|lt=1724|1=1724}}. It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in {{ill|1943|en|1943|WD=Q18623|lt=1943|1=1943}}. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from {{ill|Matsusaka|en|Matsusaka|WD=Q460829|lt=Matsusaka|1=Matsusaka}}, {{ill|Kameyama|en|Kameyama|WD=Q448668|lt=Kameyama|1=Kameyama}}, and as far as {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya|1=Nagoya}} and {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka|1=Osaka}}.Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[:en:JR Central|JR]] {{ill|Kisei Main Line|en|Kisei Main Line|WD=Q582803|lt=Kisei Main Line|1=Kisei Main Line}} {{ill|Sana Station|en|Sana Station|WD=Q265438|lt=Sana Station|1=Sana Station}}, approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. It is also accessible via {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu|1=Mie Kotsu}} bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sana Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Taki District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki District|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|qq=}} ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagasaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakaniikawa District|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefecture|qq=}}) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Togami Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomei Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakyūshū City|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}) disambiguation]] [[Tajikarao Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|佐那神社|version=103551216|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:多気郡]] [[ja:三重県]] [[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] [[ja:尼崎市]] [[ja:兵庫県]] [[ja:雄山神社]] [[ja:立山町]] [[ja:上新川郡]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:北九州市]] [[ja:福岡県]] [[ja:手力雄神社]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Sana Shrine (Q17226795)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Taki District, Mie|en|Taki District, Mie|ja|多気郡|ceb|Taki-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی تاکی|es|Distrito de Taki|fr|District de Taki|id|Distrik Taki, Mie|it|Distretto di Taki|ko|다키군|pl|Powiat Taki (Mie)|uk|Повіт Такі|zh|多氣郡|lt=Taki district|WD=Q1042360|1=Taki District, Mie}} || {{ill|Sana Shrine|lt=Sana Shrine|ja|佐那神社|WD=Q17226795|1=Sana Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || 3 seats || — || {{coord|34.480942|136.546151|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181160 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17226795}}==Notes== [[Category:Buildings and structures in Japan completed in 2015]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Taki Town]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taki, Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:pages with former wikipedia userpage links]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in {{ill|1943|en|1943|WD=Q18623|lt=1943|1=1943}}. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from {{ill|Matsusaka|en|Matsusaka|WD=Q460829|lt=Matsusaka|1=Matsusaka}}, {{ill|Kameyama|en|Kameyama|WD=Q448668|lt=Kameyama|1=Kameyama}}, and as far as {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya|1=Nagoya}} and {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka|1=Osaka}}.Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[:en:JR Central|JR]] {{ill|Kisei Main Line|en|Kisei Main Line|WD=Q582803|lt=Kisei Main Line|1=Kisei Main Line}} {{ill|Sana Station|en|Sana Station|WD=Q265438|lt=Sana Station|1=Sana Station}}, approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. It is also accessible via {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu|1=Mie Kotsu}} bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sana Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Taki District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki District|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|qq=}} ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagasaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakaniikawa District|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefecture|qq=}}) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Togami Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomei Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakyūshū City|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}) disambiguation]] [[Tajikarao Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|佐那神社|version=103551216|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:多気郡]] [[ja:三重県]] [[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] [[ja:尼崎市]] [[ja:兵庫県]] [[ja:雄山神社]] [[ja:立山町]] [[ja:上新川郡]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:北九州市]] [[ja:福岡県]] [[ja:手力雄神社]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Sana Shrine (Q17226795)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Taki District, Mie|en|Taki District, Mie|ja|多気郡|ceb|Taki-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی تاکی|es|Distrito de Taki|fr|District de Taki|id|Distrik Taki, Mie|it|Distretto di Taki|ko|다키군|pl|Powiat Taki (Mie)|uk|Повіт Такі|zh|多氣郡|lt=Taki district|WD=Q1042360|1=Taki District, Mie}} || {{ill|Sana Shrine|lt=Sana Shrine|ja|佐那神社|WD=Q17226795|1=Sana Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || 3 seats || — || {{coord|34.480942|136.546151|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181160 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17226795}}==Notes== [[Category:Buildings and structures in Japan completed in 2015]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Taki Town]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taki, Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

[[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:佐那神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sana Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Sana Shrine.jpg | location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Taki District, Mie|en|Taki District, Mie|WD=Q1042360|lt=Taki|1=Taki District, Mie}}, {{ill|Taki, Mie|en|Taki, Mie|WD=Q1203963|lt=Taki Town|1=Taki, Mie}}, Nita 156 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.480917|N|136.546139|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}}
[[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] | type = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha (Minor)]], Former Prefectural Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = [[Shimmei-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Nakamiya, Ōmori Shrine | festival = {{ill|October 8|en|October 8|WD=Q2924|lt=October 8|1=October 8}} | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Taki|WD=Q1203963|1=Taki, Mie}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-tajikarao|WD=Q849412|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Sana Shrine''' (佐那神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Taki, Mie|en|Taki, Mie|WD=Q1203963|lt=Taki Town|1=Taki, Mie}}, {{ill|Taki District, Mie|en|Taki District, Mie|WD=Q1042360|lt=Taki District|1=Taki District, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan. The shrine has historical ties to the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] and is associated with {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}}, who is also enshrined as a [[secondary deity]] at the Ise Grand Shrine. == name and Etymology == The name "Sana" originates from the place name "Sana-no-agata" mentioned in ancient texts like the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Kotaijingu Gishikicho]]''.''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 411. In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was popularly called "Ōmori Shrine" or "Nakamiya." The name "Nakamiya" is believed to relate to its status as one of three prominent shrines in the region, alongside Sumaru-me Shrine (Ichinomiya) and {{ill|Kushida |en|Kushida |WD=Q1794549|lt=Kushida Shrine|1=Kushida}} (Taisha). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the following two deities: * {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} * [[Akebono-Tatsu-O|Akebono-Tatsu-O-no-Mikoto]] Ame-no-Tajikarao is mentioned in the ''Kojiki'' as residing in "Sana-no-agata," while Akebono-Tatsu-O is described as the ancestor of the [[Sana clan]]. Additional deities from merged shrines include: * [[Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[Kumano Gongen|Hayatama-no-Mikoto]] * {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}} * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] * [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] * [[Ame-no-Hohi]] * [[Amatsuhikone]] * {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|en|Ikutsuhikone|WD=Q11147583|lt=Ikutsuhikone|1=Ikutsuhikone}} * [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano-Kusubi]] * [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Tagorihime]] * [[Ichikishima-hime]] * [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] * [[Ukanomitama]] * [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyama-tsumi]] * [[Konohanasakuya-hime]] * [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Koto-shiro-nushi]] * [[Hachiman]] * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927) as a minor shrine (小社).''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 412. The shrine's main hall has historically been rebuilt using materials donated from the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] during its [[Jingū Shikinen Sengū|Shikinen Sengu]] reconstruction. In the Edo period, the shrine received official recognition as a ''Shikinaisha'' and was referred to as "Sana Shrine" on a stone marker erected in {{ill|1724|en|1724|WD=Q6926|lt=1724|1=1724}}. It was promoted to a prefectural shrine in {{ill|1943|en|1943|WD=Q18623|lt=1943|1=1943}}. == Festivals == The shrine hosts monthly rituals on the 1st and 15th of each month, as well as annual festivals including:''Shikinaisha Kenkyukai,'' 1990, p. 413. * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 8: ''Kinen'' Festival * June 30: Summer Purification * October 8: festival * December 30: Year-End Purification == Cultural Significance == The shrine is regarded as the guardian of the former [[Sana Village]], covering several districts in modern [[Taki Town]]. The deity Ame-no-Tajikarao is worshiped as a god of strength, attracting visitors from {{ill|Matsusaka|en|Matsusaka|WD=Q460829|lt=Matsusaka|1=Matsusaka}}, {{ill|Kameyama|en|Kameyama|WD=Q448668|lt=Kameyama|1=Kameyama}}, and as far as {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya|1=Nagoya}} and {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka|1=Osaka}}.Mie Prefectural Oka High School Newspaper Club, 2011, p. 1. == Transportation == The shrine is located near [[:en:JR Central|JR]] {{ill|Kisei Main Line|en|Kisei Main Line|WD=Q582803|lt=Kisei Main Line|1=Kisei Main Line}} {{ill|Sana Station|en|Sana Station|WD=Q265438|lt=Sana Station|1=Sana Station}}, approximately a 10-minute walk away.Mie Tourism Association, accessed November 2, 2013. It is also accessible via {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu|1=Mie Kotsu}} bus from the "Gokeiguchi" stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E4%BD%90%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Sana Shrine - Mie Shrine Association (Japanese)] {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Sana Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sana Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Taki District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki District|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}) * {{ill|Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai Shrine (Amagasaki City)|qq=}} ({{ill|Amagasaki City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagasaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}) * {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Tateyama Town|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakaniikawa District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakaniikawa District|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefecture|qq=}}) * Subsidiary and branch shrines of Togakushi Shrine * {{ill|Togami Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomei Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakyūshū City|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}) disambiguation]] [[Tajikarao Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|佐那神社|version=103551216|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:佐那神社]] [[ja:多気郡]] [[ja:三重県]] [[ja:白井神社 (尼崎市)]] [[ja:尼崎市]] [[ja:兵庫県]] [[ja:雄山神社]] [[ja:立山町]] [[ja:上新川郡]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:北九州市]] [[ja:福岡県]] [[ja:手力雄神社]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] '''Sana Shrine (Q17226795)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !!
